112	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — `CombinatorialCech`
113	
114	The constant-coefficient combinatorial core of standard-cover Čech acyclicity
115	(planner step **L3**, `analogies/p3-localisation.md`).  After localising the
116	extended Čech complex at a spanning element `s_r` (planner step L2, via
117	`exact_of_isLocalized_span`), the fixed index `r` becomes globally available, and
118	the alternating Čech coboundary admits the explicit contracting homotopy
119	`h(t)_{i₀…i_p} = t_{r i₀…i_p}` of Stacks
120	`lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial`.
121	
122	This section formalises that homotopy and its consequences for the **constant
123	coefficient** complex `Cᵖ = (Fin (p+1) → ι) → M` with alternating coface
124	differential — abstracted away from the localisation/geometry so the purely
125	combinatorial cancellation (`combHomotopy`) and the simplicial identity
126	(`combDifferential_comp`, `d² = 0`) are isolated and reusable.  The remaining
127	work to close `CechAcyclic.affine` is the **L1** categorical bridge identifying
128	the abstract `CechComplex` terms with the away-localisation modules `M_{s_σ}` and
129	its differential with this `combDifferential` (after localising at `s_r`); these
130	lemmas are then fed to `exact_of_isLocalized_span` node by node.
131	
132	These declarations are `private`: they exist only to close `CechAcyclic.affine`
133	in this file.  The intended blueprint home is the `\lean{...}` bundle of
134	`lem:cech_acyclic_affine`. -/
135	
136	namespace CombinatorialCech
137	
138	variable {ι : Type*} {M : Type*} [AddCommGroup M] {n : ℕ}
139	
140	/-- Alternating coface (Čech) differential with constant coefficients in `M`:
141	`Cⁿ = (Fin n → ι) → M ⟶ Cⁿ⁺¹`, `(d t)(σ) = ∑ⱼ (-1)ʲ • t (σ ∘ j.succAbove)`.
142	The argument `σ ∘ j.succAbove` is the `(n)`-tuple obtained from the `(n+1)`-tuple
143	`σ` by deleting the `j`-th index. -/
144	private def combDifferential (t : (Fin n → ι) → M) : (Fin (n + 1) → ι) → M :=
145	  fun σ => ∑ j : Fin (n + 1), (-1 : ℤ) ^ (j : ℕ) • t (σ ∘ j.succAbove)
146	
147	/-- The contracting homotopy of the localised complex: prepend the fixed
148	(globally invertible after localising at `s_r`) index `r`.
149	`(h u)(τ) = u (Fin.cons r τ)`. -/
150	private def combHomotopy (r : ι) (u : (Fin (n + 1) → ι) → M) : (Fin n → ι) → M :=
151	  fun τ => u (Fin.cons r τ)
152	
153	@[simp] private lemma combHomotopy_zero (r : ι) :
154	    combHomotopy (M := M) (n := n) r 0 = 0 := by
155	  funext τ; simp [combHomotopy]
156	
157	/-- Composing `Fin.cons r` with the `(j+1)`-th coface map is `Fin.cons r` of the
158	`j`-th coface map: the bookkeeping identity behind the homotopy computation. -/
159	private lemma cons_comp_succAbove_succ (r : ι) (σ : Fin (n + 1) → ι) (k : Fin (n + 1)) :
160	    (Fin.cons r σ : Fin (n + 2) → ι) ∘ (k.succ).succAbove
161	      = Fin.cons r (σ ∘ k.succAbove) := by
162	  funext l
163	  refine Fin.cases ?_ ?_ l
164	  · simp
165	  · intro i; simp [Fin.succ_succAbove_succ]
166	
167	/-- **Contracting-homotopy identity** (planner L3; Stacks
168	`lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial`):
169	`d ∘ h + h ∘ d = id` on `Cⁿ⁺¹`.  This is the alternating-sum cancellation: the
170	`j = 0` term of `h (d t)` is `t`, and the remaining terms cancel against `d (h t)`
171	in pairs of opposite sign. -/
172	private lemma combHomotopy_spec (r : ι) (t : (Fin (n + 1) → ι) → M) :
173	    combDifferential (combHomotopy r t) + combHomotopy r (combDifferential t) = t := by
174	  funext σ
175	  simp only [combDifferential, combHomotopy, Pi.add_apply]
176	  rw [Fin.sum_univ_succ (f := fun j : Fin (n + 2) =>
177	    (-1 : ℤ) ^ (j : ℕ) • t ((Fin.cons r σ : Fin (n + 2) → ι) ∘ j.succAbove))]
178	  have h0 : (Fin.cons r σ : Fin (n + 2) → ι) ∘ (0 : Fin (n + 2)).succAbove = σ := by
179	    funext i; simp
180	  rw [h0]
181	  simp only [Fin.val_zero, pow_zero, one_smul, Fin.val_succ]
182	  rw [add_left_comm, ← Finset.sum_add_distrib]
183	  rw [Finset.sum_eq_zero (fun x _ => by
184	    rw [cons_comp_succAbove_succ, pow_succ, mul_comm, neg_one_mul, neg_smul]; abel), add_zero]
185	
186	/-- Every cocycle is a coboundary in positive degree: if `d t = 0` then
187	`t = d (h t)`.  This is the homological content of `combHomotopy_spec` and is the
188	half (`ker d ⊆ im d`) carrying the geometric input. -/
189	private lemma combDifferential_eq_of_cocycle (r : ι) (t : (Fin (n + 1) → ι) → M)
190	    (ht : combDifferential t = 0) : combDifferential (combHomotopy r t) = t := by
191	  have h := combHomotopy_spec r t
192	  rw [ht, combHomotopy_zero, add_zero] at h
193	  exact h
194	
195	/-- Sign-cancellation behind `d² = 0`: under the index swap
196	`(j, i) ↦ (j.succAbove i, i.predAbove j)` the alternating sign flips. -/
197	private lemma combSign_flip (j : Fin (n + 2)) (i : Fin (n + 1)) :
198	    ((-1 : ℤ) ^ (j : ℕ)) * ((-1) ^ (i : ℕ))
199	      = - (((-1 : ℤ) ^ ((j.succAbove i : Fin (n + 2)) : ℕ))
200	            * ((-1) ^ ((i.predAbove j : Fin (n + 1)) : ℕ))) := by
201	  rcases lt_or_ge (i.castSucc) j with h | h
202	  · rw [Fin.succAbove_of_castSucc_lt _ _ h, Fin.predAbove_of_castSucc_lt _ _ h,
203	        Fin.val_castSucc, Fin.val_pred]
204	    have hpos : 0 < (j : ℕ) := lt_of_le_of_lt (Nat.zero_le _) (by exact_mod_cast h)
205	    obtain ⟨m, hm⟩ : ∃ m, (j : ℕ) = m + 1 := ⟨(j : ℕ) - 1, by omega⟩
206	    rw [hm]; simp only [Nat.add_sub_cancel, pow_succ]; ring
207	  · rw [Fin.succAbove_of_le_castSucc _ _ h, Fin.predAbove_of_le_castSucc _ _ h,
208	        Fin.val_succ, Fin.coe_castPred]
209	    rw [pow_succ]; ring
210	
211	/-- **`d² = 0`** for the constant-coefficient alternating Čech complex.  Proved by
212	the standard sign-reversing involution `(j, i) ↦ (j.succAbove i, i.predAbove j)` on
213	the double sum: the swap fixes the underlying composite coface
214	(`Fin.succAbove_succAbove_succAbove_predAbove`), is an involution
215	(`Fin.succAbove_succAbove_predAbove`, `Fin.predAbove_predAbove_succAbove`), has no
216	fixed point (`Fin.succAbove_ne`), and flips the sign (`combSign_flip`). -/
217	private lemma combDifferential_comp (t : (Fin n → ι) → M) :
218	    combDifferential (combDifferential t) = 0 := by
219	  funext σ
220	  simp only [combDifferential, Pi.zero_apply, Finset.smul_sum, smul_smul]
221	  rw [← Fintype.sum_prod_type (f := fun p : Fin (n + 2) × Fin (n + 1) =>
222	    ((-1 : ℤ) ^ (p.1 : ℕ) * (-1) ^ (p.2 : ℕ)) • t ((σ ∘ p.1.succAbove) ∘ p.2.succAbove))]
223	  apply Finset.sum_involution (fun p _ => (p.1.succAbove p.2, p.2.predAbove p.1))
224	  · rintro ⟨j, i⟩ _
225	    have harg : (σ ∘ (j.succAbove i).succAbove) ∘ (i.predAbove j).succAbove
226	        = (σ ∘ j.succAbove) ∘ i.succAbove := by
227	      funext k
228	      simp only [Function.comp_apply]
229	      rw [Fin.succAbove_succAbove_succAbove_predAbove]
230	    simp only [harg]
231	    rw [← add_smul, combSign_flip j i]
232	    simp
233	  · rintro ⟨j, i⟩ _ _
234	    simp only [ne_eq, Prod.mk.injEq, not_and]
235	    intro hj
236	    exact absurd hj (Fin.succAbove_ne j i)
237	  · rintro ⟨j, i⟩ _
238	    simp only [Prod.mk.injEq]
239	    exact ⟨Fin.succAbove_succAbove_predAbove j i, Fin.predAbove_predAbove_succAbove j i⟩
240	  · intro a _; exact Finset.mem_univ _
241	
242	/-- **Positive-degree exactness** of the constant-coefficient Čech complex in the
243	`Function.Exact` form that `exact_of_isLocalized_span` (planner L2) consumes node
244	by node.  Combines `combDifferential_comp` (`im ⊆ ker`) with
245	`combDifferential_eq_of_cocycle` (`ker ⊆ im`, the homotopy half).  Requires a
246	distinguished index `r : ι` — supplied, after localising at `s_r`, by the
247	spanning element itself. -/
248	private lemma combDifferential_exact (r : ι) (n : ℕ) :
249	    Function.Exact (combDifferential : ((Fin (n + 1) → ι) → M) → ((Fin (n + 2) → ι) → M))
250	      (combDifferential : ((Fin (n + 2) → ι) → M) → ((Fin (n + 3) → ι) → M)) := by
251	  intro x
252	  constructor
253	  · intro hx
254	    exact ⟨combHomotopy r x, combDifferential_eq_of_cocycle r x hx⟩
255	  · rintro ⟨y, rfl⟩
256	    exact combDifferential_comp y
257	
258	/-! ### Dependent-coefficient port (planner **L3 port**)
259	
260	The localised complex that `exact_of_isLocalized_span` (planner **L2**) consumes
261	node-by-node has *varying* coefficients `M_{s_σ}` — the away localisation of `M`
262	at the product `s_σ = ∏ₖ s_{σ k}` — not a single constant module `M`.  After
263	localising the whole extended complex at a spanning element `s_r`, the index `r`
264	is globally available and `s_r` becomes a unit, so the prepend
265	`σ ↦ Fin.cons r σ` is an *isomorphism* on the coefficients:
266	`M_{s_{cons r σ}} = M_{s_r · s_σ} ≅ M_{s_σ}` because `s_r` is already invertible.
267	
268	This section ports the constant-coefficient cancellation
269	(`combHomotopy_spec`, `combDifferential_eq_of_cocycle`) to that dependent
270	setting.  The away-localisation restriction maps are abstracted as additive
271	coface maps `δ` (`M_{s_{σ∘dⱼ}} → M_{s_σ}`, well defined because `s_σ` carries the
272	extra factor `s_{σ j}`) and the prepend isomorphisms as additive maps `c`
273	(`M_{s_{cons r σ}} → M_{s_σ}`).  The two compatibility identities below are
274	exactly the ones the away-localisation maps satisfy:
275	
276	* the **unit** identity `c ∘ δ₀ = id` (deleting the prepended `r` from `cons r σ`
277	  recovers `σ`; on localisations the composite restriction is the identity since
278	  `s_r` is a unit), and
279	* the **shift** identity `c ∘ δ_{k+1} = δ_k ∘ c` (prepend commutes with the later
280	  cofaces), which is `cons_comp_succAbove_succ` lifted to the localisation maps.
281	
282	Threading the dependent family through the same alternating-sum cancellation as
283	the constant case then yields the dependent homotopy identity and the
284	cocycle⟹coboundary corollary that L2 consumes.  Constructing the actual `δ`/`c`
285	from `IsLocalizedModule.Away` (Mathlib `tilde` API:
286	`AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Tilde`, `IsLocalizedModule (.powers f)`) and the L1
287	identification of the abstract `CechComplex` terms with `∏_σ M_{s_σ}` remain the
288	outstanding bridge (see `CechAcyclic.affine`). -/
289	
290	section Dependent
291	
292	variable {A : (m : ℕ) → (Fin m → ι) → Type*} [∀ m σ, AddCommGroup (A m σ)]
293	variable (r : ι)
294	variable (δ : (m : ℕ) → (σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι) → (j : Fin (m + 1)) →
295	    A m (σ ∘ j.succAbove) →+ A (m + 1) σ)
296	variable (c : (m : ℕ) → (σ : Fin m → ι) → A (m + 1) (Fin.cons r σ) →+ A m σ)
297	
298	omit [∀ m σ, AddCommGroup (A m σ)] in
299	/-- Transport of a dependent cochain value along an equality of index tuples.
300	The transport friction that the constant-coefficient proof avoided (there every
301	coefficient is the same `M`) is isolated here. -/
302	private lemma depTransport {m : ℕ} {x y : Fin m → ι} (h : x = y)
303	    (t : ∀ σ : Fin m → ι, A m σ) : h ▸ t x = t y := by
304	  subst h; rfl
305	
306	/-- Deleting the prepended index `r` (the `0`-th coface of `Fin.cons r σ`)
307	recovers `σ`. -/
308	private lemma cons_comp_zero_succAbove {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin m → ι) :
309	    (Fin.cons r σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι) ∘ (0 : Fin (m + 1)).succAbove = σ := by
310	  funext i; simp
311	
312	/-- Dependent (varying-coefficient) alternating Čech differential built from the
313	coface (localisation restriction) maps `δ`. -/
314	private def depDiff {m : ℕ} (t : ∀ σ : Fin m → ι, A m σ) :
315	    ∀ σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι, A (m + 1) σ :=
316	  fun σ => ∑ j : Fin (m + 1), (-1 : ℤ) ^ (j : ℕ) • δ m σ j (t (σ ∘ j.succAbove))
317	
318	/-- Dependent contracting homotopy: prepend the distinguished index `r`, then
319	apply the prepend map `c`. -/
320	private def depHomotopy {m : ℕ} (u : ∀ σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι, A (m + 1) σ) :
321	    ∀ σ : Fin m → ι, A m σ :=
322	  fun σ => c m σ (u (Fin.cons r σ))
323	
324	/-- **Dependent contracting-homotopy identity** (planner L3 port): `d ∘ h + h ∘ d
325	= id`, evaluated at a tuple `σ`.  Same alternating-sum cancellation as
326	`combHomotopy_spec`, threaded through the varying coefficients via the unit
327	identity `hu` (`c ∘ δ₀ = id`) and the shift identity `hsh`
328	(`c ∘ δ_{k+1} = δ_k ∘ c`). -/
329	private lemma depHomotopy_spec
330	    (hu : ∀ {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι)
331	        (y : A (m + 1)
332	          ((Fin.cons r σ : Fin (m + 2) → ι) ∘ (0 : Fin (m + 2)).succAbove)),
333	        c (m + 1) σ (δ (m + 1) (Fin.cons r σ) 0 y)
334	          = (cons_comp_zero_succAbove r σ) ▸ y)
335	    (hsh : ∀ {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι) (k : Fin (m + 1))
336	        (y : A (m + 1)
337	          ((Fin.cons r σ : Fin (m + 2) → ι) ∘ (k.succ).succAbove)),
338	        c (m + 1) σ (δ (m + 1) (Fin.cons r σ) (k.succ) y)
339	          = δ m σ k (c m (σ ∘ k.succAbove) ((cons_comp_succAbove_succ r σ k) ▸ y)))
340	    {m : ℕ} (t : ∀ σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι, A (m + 1) σ) (σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι) :
341	    depDiff δ (depHomotopy r c t) σ + depHomotopy r c (depDiff δ t) σ = t σ := by
342	  simp only [depDiff, depHomotopy]
343	  rw [map_sum]
344	  simp only [map_zsmul]
345	  rw [Fin.sum_univ_succ (f := fun k : Fin (m + 2) =>
346	    (-1 : ℤ) ^ (k : ℕ) • c (m + 1) σ
347	      (δ (m + 1) (Fin.cons r σ) k (t ((Fin.cons r σ : Fin (m + 2) → ι) ∘ k.succAbove))))]
348	  -- the `k = 0` term collapses to `t σ` via the unit identity
349	  rw [hu σ (t _)]
350	  rw [depTransport (cons_comp_zero_succAbove r σ) t]
351	  simp only [Fin.val_zero, pow_zero, one_smul, Fin.val_succ]
352	  -- the `k = j.succ` terms cancel against the `d ∘ h` sum in pairs of opposite sign
353	  rw [add_left_comm, ← Finset.sum_add_distrib]
354	  rw [Finset.sum_eq_zero (fun x _ => by
355	    rw [hsh σ x (t _), depTransport (cons_comp_succAbove_succ r σ x) t,
356	      pow_succ, mul_comm, neg_one_mul, neg_smul]
357	    abel), add_zero]
358	
359	/-- Dependent cocycle⟹coboundary (the geometric half consumed by L2): if
360	`depDiff t = 0` then `t = depDiff (depHomotopy t)`. -/
361	private lemma depDiff_eq_of_cocycle
362	    (hu : ∀ {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι)
363	        (y : A (m + 1)
364	          ((Fin.cons r σ : Fin (m + 2) → ι) ∘ (0 : Fin (m + 2)).succAbove)),
365	        c (m + 1) σ (δ (m + 1) (Fin.cons r σ) 0 y)
366	          = (cons_comp_zero_succAbove r σ) ▸ y)
367	    (hsh : ∀ {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι) (k : Fin (m + 1))
368	        (y : A (m + 1)
369	          ((Fin.cons r σ : Fin (m + 2) → ι) ∘ (k.succ).succAbove)),
370	        c (m + 1) σ (δ (m + 1) (Fin.cons r σ) (k.succ) y)
371	          = δ m σ k (c m (σ ∘ k.succAbove) ((cons_comp_succAbove_succ r σ k) ▸ y)))
372	    {m : ℕ} (t : ∀ σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι, A (m + 1) σ)
373	    (ht : depDiff δ t = 0) (σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι) :
374	    depDiff δ (depHomotopy r c t) σ = t σ := by
375	  have h := depHomotopy_spec r δ c hu hsh t σ
376	  rw [show depHomotopy r c (depDiff δ t) σ = 0 by rw [ht]; simp [depHomotopy], add_zero] at h
377	  exact h
378	
379	omit [∀ m σ, AddCommGroup (A m σ)] in
380	/-- The composite coface tuple is symmetric under the `d²=0` index swap
381	`(j, i) ↦ (j.succAbove i, i.predAbove j)` (dependent analogue of the `harg` step
382	inside `combDifferential_comp`). -/
383	private lemma comp_succAbove_swap {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin (m + 2) → ι)
384	    (j : Fin (m + 2)) (i : Fin (m + 1)) :
385	    (σ ∘ (j.succAbove i).succAbove) ∘ (i.predAbove j).succAbove
386	      = (σ ∘ j.succAbove) ∘ i.succAbove := by
387	  funext k
388	  simp only [Function.comp_apply]
389	  rw [Fin.succAbove_succAbove_succAbove_predAbove]
390	
391	/-- **Dependent `d² = 0`** for the varying-coefficient alternating Čech complex.
392	Same sign-reversing involution `(j, i) ↦ (j.succAbove i, i.predAbove j)` as
393	`combDifferential_comp`, with the coefficient transport handled by
394	`comp_succAbove_swap` and the coface-commutation hypothesis `hcomm` (the two
395	restriction maps into `M_{s_σ}` agree — a formal property of localisation maps,
396	independent of the choice of `r`). -/
397	private lemma depDiff_comp
398	    (hcomm : ∀ {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin (m + 2) → ι) (j : Fin (m + 2)) (i : Fin (m + 1))
399	        (z : A m ((σ ∘ j.succAbove) ∘ i.succAbove)),
400	        δ (m + 1) σ j (δ m (σ ∘ j.succAbove) i z)
401	          = δ (m + 1) σ (j.succAbove i)
402	              (δ m (σ ∘ (j.succAbove i).succAbove) (i.predAbove j)
403	                ((comp_succAbove_swap σ j i).symm ▸ z)))
404	    {m : ℕ} (t : ∀ σ : Fin m → ι, A m σ) :
405	    depDiff δ (depDiff δ t) = 0 := by
406	  funext σ
407	  simp only [depDiff, Pi.zero_apply, map_sum, map_zsmul, Finset.smul_sum, smul_smul]
408	  rw [← Fintype.sum_prod_type (f := fun p : Fin (m + 2) × Fin (m + 1) =>
409	    ((-1 : ℤ) ^ (p.1 : ℕ) * (-1) ^ (p.2 : ℕ)) •
410	      δ (m + 1) σ p.1 (δ m (σ ∘ p.1.succAbove) p.2 (t ((σ ∘ p.1.succAbove) ∘ p.2.succAbove))))]
411	  apply Finset.sum_involution (fun p _ => (p.1.succAbove p.2, p.2.predAbove p.1))
412	  · rintro ⟨j, i⟩ _
413	    simp only []
414	    rw [hcomm σ j i (t _), depTransport (comp_succAbove_swap σ j i).symm t,
415	      ← add_smul, combSign_flip j i]
416	    simp
417	  · rintro ⟨j, i⟩ _ _
418	    simp only [ne_eq, Prod.mk.injEq, not_and]
419	    intro hj
420	    exact absurd hj (Fin.succAbove_ne j i)
421	  · rintro ⟨j, i⟩ _
422	    simp only [Prod.mk.injEq]
423	    exact ⟨Fin.succAbove_succAbove_predAbove j i, Fin.predAbove_predAbove_succAbove j i⟩
424	  · intro a _; exact Finset.mem_univ _
425	
426	/-- **Dependent positive-degree exactness** in the `Function.Exact` form that
427	`exact_of_isLocalized_span` (planner **L2**) consumes after localising at a
428	spanning element `s_r`.  Combines `depDiff_comp` (`im ⊆ ker`) with
429	`depDiff_eq_of_cocycle` (`ker ⊆ im`, the homotopy half carrying the
430	`s_r`-invertibility input).  This is the dependent-coefficient analogue of
431	`combDifferential_exact`. -/
432	private lemma depDiff_exact
433	    (hu : ∀ {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι)
434	        (y : A (m + 1)
435	          ((Fin.cons r σ : Fin (m + 2) → ι) ∘ (0 : Fin (m + 2)).succAbove)),
436	        c (m + 1) σ (δ (m + 1) (Fin.cons r σ) 0 y)
437	          = (cons_comp_zero_succAbove r σ) ▸ y)
438	    (hsh : ∀ {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι) (k : Fin (m + 1))
439	        (y : A (m + 1)
440	          ((Fin.cons r σ : Fin (m + 2) → ι) ∘ (k.succ).succAbove)),
441	        c (m + 1) σ (δ (m + 1) (Fin.cons r σ) (k.succ) y)
442	          = δ m σ k (c m (σ ∘ k.succAbove) ((cons_comp_succAbove_succ r σ k) ▸ y)))
443	    (hcomm : ∀ {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin (m + 2) → ι) (j : Fin (m + 2)) (i : Fin (m + 1))
444	        (z : A m ((σ ∘ j.succAbove) ∘ i.succAbove)),
445	        δ (m + 1) σ j (δ m (σ ∘ j.succAbove) i z)
446	          = δ (m + 1) σ (j.succAbove i)
447	              (δ m (σ ∘ (j.succAbove i).succAbove) (i.predAbove j)
448	                ((comp_succAbove_swap σ j i).symm ▸ z)))
449	    (m : ℕ) :
450	    Function.Exact (depDiff δ (m := m + 1)) (depDiff δ (m := m + 2)) := by
451	  intro x
452	  constructor
453	  · intro hx
454	    exact ⟨depHomotopy r c x,
455	      funext fun σ => depDiff_eq_of_cocycle r δ c hu hsh x hx σ⟩
456	  · rintro ⟨y, rfl⟩
457	    exact depDiff_comp δ hcomm y
458	
459	end Dependent
460	
461	end CombinatorialCech
462	
463	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — away-localisation comparison maps
464	
465	The concrete `δ` (coface) and `c` (prepend) maps that the dependent combinatorial
466	core `CombinatorialCech.Dependent.depDiff_exact` consumes are, for the standard
467	affine cover, the canonical comparison maps between away-localisations
468	`M_a → M_b` available whenever `a ∣ b` — so that `a`, a divisor of the invertible
469	`b`, is itself invertible in `M_b`.  This section builds that comparison map and
470	its functoriality from `IsLocalizedModule` abstractly, independent of the sheaf
471	identification (L1), so it can be assembled into the localised {\v C}ech complex.
472	
473	Everything here is `R`-module algebra; no sheaf theory enters. The maps are
474	characterised by uniqueness of localisation lifts (`IsLocalizedModule.lift_unique`),
475	which is what makes the composition/identity laws — the algebraic heart of the
476	`hu`/`hsh`/`hcomm` compatibilities — provable. -/
477	
478	namespace AwayComparison
479	
480	variable {R : Type u} [CommRing R] {M : Type u} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M]
481	
482	/-- The clean hypothesis under which a canonical comparison `M_a → Mb` exists: the
483	element `a` acts invertibly on the target localisation `Mb`. Because the scalar
484	action is `algebraMap` into the (central) endomorphism ring, this is closed under
485	multiplication and holds for any divisor of an already-invertible element — exactly
486	the two ways `δ` (divisibility) and `c` (a product of invertible factors) arise. -/
487	def Inverts (a : R) (Mb : Type u) [AddCommGroup Mb] [Module R Mb] : Prop :=
488	  IsUnit (algebraMap R (Module.End R Mb) a)
489	
490	/-- A divisor `a` of `b` acts invertibly on any localisation `M_b` of `M` away
491	from `b`: since `b` is a unit there and `b = a · c` with the scalar actions
492	commuting (both lie in the image of the central `algebraMap`), `a` is a unit too. -/
493	lemma Inverts.of_dvd {a b : R} (hab : a ∣ b)
494	    {Mb : Type u} [AddCommGroup Mb] [Module R Mb] (fb : M →ₗ[R] Mb)
495	    [IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers b) fb] :
496	    Inverts a Mb := by
497	  obtain ⟨c, rfl⟩ := hab
498	  have hb : IsUnit (algebraMap R (Module.End R Mb) (a * c)) :=
499	    IsLocalizedModule.map_units fb ⟨a * c, Submonoid.mem_powers _⟩
500	  rw [map_mul] at hb
501	  have hcomm : Commute (algebraMap R (Module.End R Mb) a) (algebraMap R (Module.End R Mb) c) :=
502	    (Commute.all a c).map _
503	  exact (hcomm.isUnit_mul_iff.mp hb).1
504	
505	/-- `Inverts` is closed under multiplication: a product of invertibly-acting
506	elements acts invertibly. This supplies the `c` (prepend) comparison, whose source
507	localising element `s_r · s_{cons r σ}` factors into divisors of the target. -/
508	lemma Inverts.mul {a a' : R} {Mb : Type u} [AddCommGroup Mb] [Module R Mb]
509	    (ha : Inverts a Mb) (ha' : Inverts a' Mb) : Inverts (a * a') Mb := by
510	  rw [Inverts, map_mul]
511	  exact IsUnit.mul ha ha'
512	
513	/-- The power version of the `Inverts` hypothesis, as the `IsLocalizedModule.lift`
514	universal property consumes it (`∀ x ∈ powers a`). -/
515	lemma Inverts.isUnit_powers {a : R} {Mb : Type u} [AddCommGroup Mb] [Module R Mb]
516	    (ha : Inverts a Mb) (x : Submonoid.powers a) :
517	    IsUnit (algebraMap R (Module.End R Mb) (x : R)) := by
518	  obtain ⟨n, hn⟩ := x.2
519	  rw [← hn, map_pow]
520	  exact ha.pow n
521	
522	/-- The canonical comparison map `M_a → Mb` between localisations of `M`, defined
523	whenever `a` acts invertibly on `Mb` (`Inverts a Mb`). It is the unique `R`-linear
524	map `M_a → Mb` commuting with the localisation structure maps (`comparison_unique`);
525	this universal characterisation is what makes the functoriality laws below provable. -/
526	noncomputable def comparison {a : R}
527	    {Ma Mb : Type u} [AddCommGroup Ma] [Module R Ma] [AddCommGroup Mb] [Module R Mb]
528	    (fa : M →ₗ[R] Ma) [IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers a) fa]
529	    (fb : M →ₗ[R] Mb) (hb : Inverts a Mb) :
530	    Ma →ₗ[R] Mb :=
531	  IsLocalizedModule.lift (Submonoid.powers a) fa fb hb.isUnit_powers
532	
533	@[simp] lemma comparison_apply {a : R}
534	    {Ma Mb : Type u} [AddCommGroup Ma] [Module R Ma] [AddCommGroup Mb] [Module R Mb]
535	    (fa : M →ₗ[R] Ma) [IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers a) fa]
536	    (fb : M →ₗ[R] Mb) (hb : Inverts a Mb) (x : M) :
537	    comparison fa fb hb (fa x) = fb x :=
538	  IsLocalizedModule.lift_apply _ _ _ _ x
539	
540	lemma comparison_comp_structure {a : R}
541	    {Ma Mb : Type u} [AddCommGroup Ma] [Module R Ma] [AddCommGroup Mb] [Module R Mb]
542	    (fa : M →ₗ[R] Ma) [IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers a) fa]
543	    (fb : M →ₗ[R] Mb) (hb : Inverts a Mb) :
544	    comparison fa fb hb ∘ₗ fa = fb :=
545	  IsLocalizedModule.lift_comp _ _ _ _
546	
547	/-- Uniqueness: any `R`-linear `l : M_a → Mb` commuting with the structure maps
548	equals the comparison map. -/
549	lemma comparison_unique {a : R}
550	    {Ma Mb : Type u} [AddCommGroup Ma] [Module R Ma] [AddCommGroup Mb] [Module R Mb]
551	    (fa : M →ₗ[R] Ma) [IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers a) fa]
552	    (fb : M →ₗ[R] Mb) (hb : Inverts a Mb)
553	    (l : Ma →ₗ[R] Mb) (hl : l ∘ₗ fa = fb) :
554	    comparison fa fb hb = l :=
555	  IsLocalizedModule.lift_unique _ _ _ _ l hl
556	
557	/-- **Identity law.** The comparison of a localisation with itself is the identity. -/
558	@[simp] lemma comparison_self {a : R}
559	    {Ma : Type u} [AddCommGroup Ma] [Module R Ma]
560	    (fa : M →ₗ[R] Ma) [IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers a) fa] (ha : Inverts a Ma) :
561	    comparison fa fa ha = LinearMap.id :=
562	  comparison_unique _ _ _ _ (by ext x; simp)
563	
564	/-- **Composition law** (functoriality): comparison maps compose.  Proved by
565	uniqueness — both sides become `fc` after precomposing with the structure map `fa`.
566	This is the reusable algebraic core underlying the `hsh`/`hcomm` compatibilities of
567	the dependent {\v C}ech port. -/
568	lemma comparison_comp {a b : R}
569	    {Ma Mb Mc : Type u} [AddCommGroup Ma] [Module R Ma] [AddCommGroup Mb] [Module R Mb]
570	    [AddCommGroup Mc] [Module R Mc]
571	    (fa : M →ₗ[R] Ma) [IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers a) fa]
572	    (fb : M →ₗ[R] Mb) [IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers b) fb]
573	    (fc : M →ₗ[R] Mc)
574	    (hb : Inverts a Mb) (hc : Inverts b Mc) (hac : Inverts a Mc) :
575	    (comparison fb fc hc) ∘ₗ (comparison fa fb hb) = comparison fa fc hac :=
576	  (comparison_unique fa fc hac _ (by
577	    ext x
578	    simp only [LinearMap.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply, comparison_apply])).symm
579	
580	/-- Pointwise form of the composition law. -/
581	lemma comparison_comp_apply {a b : R}
582	    {Ma Mb Mc : Type u} [AddCommGroup Ma] [Module R Ma] [AddCommGroup Mb] [Module R Mb]
583	    [AddCommGroup Mc] [Module R Mc]
584	    (fa : M →ₗ[R] Ma) [IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers a) fa]
585	    (fb : M →ₗ[R] Mb) [IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers b) fb]
586	    (fc : M →ₗ[R] Mc)
587	    (hb : Inverts a Mb) (hc : Inverts b Mc) (hac : Inverts a Mc) (x : Ma) :
588	    comparison fb fc hc (comparison fa fb hb x) = comparison fa fc hac x :=
589	  LinearMap.congr_fun (comparison_comp fa fb fc hb hc hac) x
590	
591	/-- A power of an invertibly-acting element acts injectively on a localisation:
592	the scalar-cancellation device behind localisation transitivity. -/
593	lemma Inverts.smul_pow_cancel {a : R} {N : Type u} [AddCommGroup N] [Module R N]
594	    (ha : Inverts a N) (n : ℕ) {p q : N} (h : a ^ n • p = a ^ n • q) : p = q := by
595	  have hu : IsUnit (algebraMap R (Module.End R N) (a ^ n)) := by
596	    rw [map_pow]; exact ha.pow n
597	  apply ((Module.End.isUnit_iff _).mp hu).injective
598	  simpa only [Module.algebraMap_end_eq_smul_id, LinearMap.smul_apply, LinearMap.id_coe,
599	    id_eq] using h
600	
601	/-- **Localisation transitivity for the away comparison.** If `Ma` localises `M`
602	away from `a` and `Mb` away from `a * b`, the comparison map `Ma → Mb` exhibits
603	`Mb` as the localisation of `Ma` away from `b` — the "localisation of a
604	localisation" identity `M_a[1/b] = M_{ab}`.  This is the keystone that feeds the
605	per-spanning-element reduction `exact_of_isLocalized_span` of the section Čech
606	complex: localising the un-localised coefficient `M_{s_σ}` at a spanning element
607	`s_r` yields the doubly-localised coefficient `M_{s_r · s_σ}` of `cechCoeff`. -/
608	lemma comparison_isLocalizedModule {a b : R}
609	    {Ma Mb : Type u} [AddCommGroup Ma] [Module R Ma] [AddCommGroup Mb] [Module R Mb]
610	    (fa : M →ₗ[R] Ma) [IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers a) fa]
611	    (fb : M →ₗ[R] Mb) [IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (a * b)) fb]
612	    (hb : Inverts a Mb) :
613	    IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers b) (comparison fa fb hb) := by
614	  have hbB : Inverts b Mb := Inverts.of_dvd ⟨a, by rw [mul_comm]⟩ fb
615	  have haA : Inverts a Ma := Inverts.of_dvd dvd_rfl fa
616	  refine ⟨fun x => hbB.isUnit_powers x, ?_, ?_⟩
617	  · -- surjectivity
618	    intro y
619	    obtain ⟨⟨m, u⟩, hu⟩ := IsLocalizedModule.surj (Submonoid.powers (a * b)) fb y
620	    obtain ⟨n, hn⟩ := u.2
621	    have hnR : (a * b) ^ n = (u : R) := hn
622	    refine ⟨⟨IsLocalizedModule.mk' fa m (⟨a ^ n, n, rfl⟩ : Submonoid.powers a),
623	      (⟨b ^ n, n, rfl⟩ : Submonoid.powers b)⟩, ?_⟩
624	    apply hb.smul_pow_cancel n
625	    -- RHS: a^n • comparison (mk' fa m ⟨a^n⟩) = fb m
626	    have hz : (a ^ n : R) • IsLocalizedModule.mk' fa m (⟨a ^ n, n, rfl⟩ : Submonoid.powers a)
627	        = fa m := by
628	      have h := IsLocalizedModule.mk'_cancel' fa m (⟨a ^ n, n, rfl⟩ : Submonoid.powers a)
629	      rwa [Submonoid.smul_def] at h
630	    rw [Submonoid.smul_def, ← _root_.map_smul, hz, comparison_apply]
631	    -- LHS: a^n • (b^n • y) = fb m
632	    rw [smul_smul, ← mul_pow, hnR, ← Submonoid.smul_def]
633	    exact hu
634	  · -- exists_of_eq
635	    intro x₁ x₂ e
636	    set w := x₁ - x₂ with hw
637	    have hcw : comparison fa fb hb w = 0 := by rw [hw, map_sub, e, sub_self]
638	    obtain ⟨⟨m, s⟩, hs⟩ := IsLocalizedModule.surj (Submonoid.powers a) fa w
639	    obtain ⟨k, hk⟩ := s.2
640	    have hsw : a ^ k • w = fa m := by
641	      rw [Submonoid.smul_def] at hs
642	      rwa [show (s : R) = a ^ k from hk.symm] at hs
643	    have hfbm : fb m = 0 := by
644	      have h1 : comparison fa fb hb (fa m) = 0 := by
645	        rw [← hsw, _root_.map_smul, hcw, smul_zero]
646	      rwa [comparison_apply] at h1
647	    have hmz : fb m = fb 0 := by rw [hfbm, map_zero]
648	    obtain ⟨d, hd⟩ := (IsLocalizedModule.eq_iff_exists (Submonoid.powers (a * b)) fb).mp hmz
649	    obtain ⟨j, hj⟩ := d.2
650	    have hdm : (a * b) ^ j • m = 0 := by
651	      rw [smul_zero] at hd
652	      rw [Submonoid.smul_def] at hd
653	      rwa [show (d : R) = (a * b) ^ j from hj.symm] at hd
654	    -- a^{j+k} • (b^j • w) = 0
655	    have key : (a * b) ^ j • (a ^ k • w) = (0 : Ma) := by
656	      rw [hsw, ← _root_.map_smul, hdm, map_zero]
657	    have hzero : a ^ (j + k) • (b ^ j • w) = (0 : Ma) := by
658	      rw [smul_smul] at key
659	      rw [smul_smul, show a ^ (j + k) * b ^ j = (a * b) ^ j * a ^ k by
660	        rw [mul_pow, pow_add]; ring]
661	      exact key
662	    have hbw : b ^ j • w = 0 :=
663	      haA.smul_pow_cancel (j + k) (hzero.trans (smul_zero _).symm)
664	    refine ⟨(⟨b ^ j, j, rfl⟩ : Submonoid.powers b), ?_⟩
665	    rw [Submonoid.smul_def, Submonoid.smul_def]
666	    rw [hw, smul_sub, sub_eq_zero] at hbw
667	    exact hbw
668	
669	end AwayComparison
670	
671	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — multi-index localising elements
672	
673	The localising element attached to a {\v C}ech multi-index `σ : Fin m → ι` is the
674	product `s_σ = ∏ₖ s (σ k)`.  The two divisibility facts below are exactly the
675	hypotheses the away-comparison maps of `AwayComparison` need to become the
676	coface `δ` and prepend `c` maps of the dependent combinatorial core: a coface
677	deletes an index (so `s_{σ∘dⱼ} ∣ s_σ`), and the prepend multiplies by `s r`. -/
678	
679	namespace CechLocalized
680	
681	variable {R : Type u} [CommRing R] {ι : Type*} (s : ι → R)
682	
683	/-- `s_σ = ∏ₖ s (σ k)`, the localising element for the multi-index `σ`. -/
684	def sprod {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin m → ι) : R := ∏ k, s (σ k)
685	
686	@[simp] lemma sprod_cons {m : ℕ} (i : ι) (σ : Fin m → ι) :
687	    sprod s (Fin.cons i σ) = s i * sprod s σ := by
688	  simp [sprod, Fin.prod_univ_succ]
689	
690	/-- A coface (index deletion) only drops the factor `s (σ j)`, so the smaller
691	product divides the larger: `s_{σ∘dⱼ} ∣ s_σ`.  This supplies the `Inverts`
692	hypothesis for the coface comparison `δ`. -/
693	lemma sprod_succAbove_dvd {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι) (j : Fin (m + 1)) :
694	    sprod s (σ ∘ j.succAbove) ∣ sprod s σ := by
695	  have h : sprod s σ = s (σ j) * sprod s (σ ∘ j.succAbove) := by
696	    rw [sprod, sprod, Fin.prod_univ_succAbove _ j]; rfl
697	  exact ⟨s (σ j), by rw [h]; ring⟩
698	
699	/-! ### The localised coefficient family and the concrete `δ`/`c` maps
700	
701	After localising the whole section {\v C}ech complex at a spanning element `s r`,
702	its degree-`p` coefficient at the multi-index `σ` is `M` localised at the product
703	`s r · s_σ` (the away localisation at `s_σ`, further localised at `s r`).  We model
704	that double localisation by the single away localisation at `s r · s_σ`.  The coface
705	`δ` and prepend `c` maps are the away-comparison maps of `AwayComparison`; the
706	`Inverts` hypotheses come from `sprod_succAbove_dvd` (for `δ`) and from
707	`Inverts.mul` together with `sprod_cons` (for `c`). -/
708	
709	variable (M : Type u) [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] (r : ι)
710	
711	/-- The localised {\v C}ech coefficient `A_σ = M_{s r · s_σ}` (after localising at the
712	fixed index `r`). -/
713	abbrev cechCoeff {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin m → ι) : Type u :=
714	  LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (s r * sprod s σ)) M
715	
716	/-- The coface comparison `δ : A_{σ∘dⱼ} → A_σ`, the canonical localisation map for the
717	divisibility `s r · s_{σ∘dⱼ} ∣ s r · s_σ`. -/
718	noncomputable def cechCoface (m : ℕ) (σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι) (j : Fin (m + 1)) :
719	    cechCoeff s M r (σ ∘ j.succAbove) →+ cechCoeff s M r σ :=
720	  (AwayComparison.comparison (M := M)
721	    (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (s r * sprod s (σ ∘ j.succAbove))) M)
722	    (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (s r * sprod s σ)) M)
723	    (AwayComparison.Inverts.of_dvd (mul_dvd_mul_left (s r) (sprod_succAbove_dvd s σ j))
724	      (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (s r * sprod s σ)) M))).toAddMonoidHom
725	
726	/-- The prepend comparison `c : A_{cons r σ} → A_σ`, the canonical localisation map.
727	Its source localises at `s r · s_{cons r σ} = s r · (s r · s_σ)`, a product whose
728	factors are all invertible in `A_σ`, supplied by `Inverts.mul`. -/
729	noncomputable def cechPrepend (m : ℕ) (σ : Fin m → ι) :
730	    cechCoeff s M r (Fin.cons r σ) →+ cechCoeff s M r σ :=
731	  (AwayComparison.comparison (M := M)
732	    (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (s r * sprod s (Fin.cons r σ))) M)
733	    (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (s r * sprod s σ)) M)
734	    (by
735	      change AwayComparison.Inverts (s r * sprod s (Fin.cons r σ))
736	        (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (s r * sprod s σ)) M)
737	      have h : s r * sprod s (Fin.cons r σ) = s r * (s r * sprod s σ) := by
738	        rw [sprod_cons]
739	      rw [h]
740	      exact AwayComparison.Inverts.mul
741	        (AwayComparison.Inverts.of_dvd ⟨sprod s σ, rfl⟩
742	          (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (s r * sprod s σ)) M))
743	        (AwayComparison.Inverts.of_dvd dvd_rfl
744	          (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (s r * sprod s σ)) M)))).toAddMonoidHom
745	
746	/-- A transport of a {\v C}ech coefficient along an equality of multi-index tuples
747	`τ₁ = τ₂` is the canonical comparison map (both localise `M` at the *same* element
748	`s r · s_τ`, since `s_τ` depends only on the function `τ`). This is the bridge
749	between the dependent-type transports `▸` of the combinatorial core and the
750	away-comparison maps. -/
751	lemma cechCoeff_transport_eq_comparison {m : ℕ} {τ₁ τ₂ : Fin m → ι} (hτ : τ₁ = τ₂)
752	    (hinv : AwayComparison.Inverts (s r * sprod s τ₁) (cechCoeff s M r τ₂))
753	    (y : cechCoeff s M r τ₁) :
754	    hτ ▸ y = AwayComparison.comparison
755	      (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (s r * sprod s τ₁)) M)
756	      (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (s r * sprod s τ₂)) M) hinv y := by
757	  subst hτ
758	  rw [AwayComparison.comparison_self]
759	  rfl
760	
761	/-- **Unit compatibility** `hu` for the concrete localised {\v C}ech maps:
762	`c ∘ δ₀ = transport`.  Deleting the prepended `r` (the `0`-th coface) and then
763	applying the prepend `c` is, on the away localisations, the identity transport,
764	because all three maps are away-comparison maps and compose by `comparison_comp`. -/
765	lemma cech_hu {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι)
766	    (y : cechCoeff s M r ((Fin.cons r σ : Fin (m + 2) → ι) ∘ (0 : Fin (m + 2)).succAbove)) :
767	    cechPrepend s M r (m + 1) σ (cechCoface s M r (m + 1) (Fin.cons r σ) 0 y)
768	      = (CombinatorialCech.cons_comp_zero_succAbove r σ) ▸ y := by
769	  have heq : (Fin.cons r σ : Fin (m + 2) → ι) ∘ (0 : Fin (m + 2)).succAbove = σ :=
770	    CombinatorialCech.cons_comp_zero_succAbove r σ
771	  have hinv : AwayComparison.Inverts
772	      (s r * sprod s ((Fin.cons r σ : Fin (m + 2) → ι) ∘ (0 : Fin (m + 2)).succAbove))
773	      (cechCoeff s M r σ) :=
774	    AwayComparison.Inverts.of_dvd (dvd_of_eq (by rw [heq])) (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap _ M)
775	  simp only [cechPrepend, cechCoface, LinearMap.toAddMonoidHom_coe]
776	  change _ = heq ▸ y
777	  rw [cechCoeff_transport_eq_comparison s M r heq hinv,
778	    AwayComparison.comparison_comp_apply]
779	
780	/-- **Shift compatibility** `hsh` for the concrete localised {\v C}ech maps:
781	`c ∘ δ_{k+1} = δ_k ∘ c` (modulo the index transport).  Both composites are
782	away-comparison maps from the same source localisation to the same target, hence
783	equal — assembled from `comparison_comp` and the transport bridge. -/
784	lemma cech_hsh {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι) (k : Fin (m + 1))
785	    (y : cechCoeff s M r ((Fin.cons r σ : Fin (m + 2) → ι) ∘ (k.succ).succAbove)) :
786	    cechPrepend s M r (m + 1) σ (cechCoface s M r (m + 1) (Fin.cons r σ) k.succ y)
787	      = cechCoface s M r m σ k
788	          (cechPrepend s M r m (σ ∘ k.succAbove)
789	            ((CombinatorialCech.cons_comp_succAbove_succ r σ k) ▸ y)) := by
790	  have heq' : (Fin.cons r σ : Fin (m + 2) → ι) ∘ (k.succ).succAbove
791	      = Fin.cons r (σ ∘ k.succAbove) := CombinatorialCech.cons_comp_succAbove_succ r σ k
792	  have hinv' : AwayComparison.Inverts
793	      (s r * sprod s ((Fin.cons r σ : Fin (m + 2) → ι) ∘ (k.succ).succAbove))
794	      (cechCoeff s M r (Fin.cons r (σ ∘ k.succAbove))) :=
795	    AwayComparison.Inverts.of_dvd (dvd_of_eq (by rw [heq'])) (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap _ M)
796	  have key : AwayComparison.Inverts (s r * sprod s (Fin.cons r (σ ∘ k.succAbove)))
797	      (cechCoeff s M r σ) := by
798	    rw [show s r * sprod s (Fin.cons r (σ ∘ k.succAbove))
799	          = s r * (s r * sprod s (σ ∘ k.succAbove)) from by rw [sprod_cons]]
800	    exact AwayComparison.Inverts.mul
801	      (AwayComparison.Inverts.of_dvd (dvd_mul_right (s r) (sprod s σ))
802	        (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap _ M))
803	      (AwayComparison.Inverts.of_dvd (mul_dvd_mul_left (s r) (sprod_succAbove_dvd s σ k))
804	        (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap _ M))
805	  simp only [cechPrepend, cechCoface, LinearMap.toAddMonoidHom_coe]
806	  rw [cechCoeff_transport_eq_comparison s M r heq' hinv',
807	    AwayComparison.comparison_comp_apply, AwayComparison.comparison_comp_apply,
808	    AwayComparison.comparison_comp_apply]
809	  · rw [heq']; exact key
810	  · exact key
811	
812	/-- **Coface commutation** `hcomm` (the `d² = 0` swap identity) for the concrete
813	localised {\v C}ech maps.  Both bracketings of the double coface are away-comparison
814	maps from the (swap-invariant) double-deletion localisation to `A_σ`, hence equal. -/
815	lemma cech_hcomm {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin (m + 2) → ι) (j : Fin (m + 2)) (i : Fin (m + 1))
816	    (z : cechCoeff s M r ((σ ∘ j.succAbove) ∘ i.succAbove)) :
817	    cechCoface s M r (m + 1) σ j (cechCoface s M r m (σ ∘ j.succAbove) i z)
818	      = cechCoface s M r (m + 1) σ (j.succAbove i)
819	          (cechCoface s M r m (σ ∘ (j.succAbove i).succAbove) (i.predAbove j)
820	            ((CombinatorialCech.comp_succAbove_swap σ j i).symm ▸ z)) := by
821	  have heqc : (σ ∘ j.succAbove) ∘ i.succAbove
822	      = (σ ∘ (j.succAbove i).succAbove) ∘ (i.predAbove j).succAbove :=
823	    (CombinatorialCech.comp_succAbove_swap σ j i).symm
824	  have hinvc : AwayComparison.Inverts (s r * sprod s ((σ ∘ j.succAbove) ∘ i.succAbove))
825	      (cechCoeff s M r ((σ ∘ (j.succAbove i).succAbove) ∘ (i.predAbove j).succAbove)) :=
826	    AwayComparison.Inverts.of_dvd (dvd_of_eq (by rw [heqc])) (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap _ M)
827	  have key : AwayComparison.Inverts (s r * sprod s ((σ ∘ j.succAbove) ∘ i.succAbove))
828	      (cechCoeff s M r σ) :=
829	    AwayComparison.Inverts.of_dvd
830	      (mul_dvd_mul_left (s r)
831	        (dvd_trans (sprod_succAbove_dvd s (σ ∘ j.succAbove) i) (sprod_succAbove_dvd s σ j)))
832	      (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap _ M)
833	  simp only [cechCoface, LinearMap.toAddMonoidHom_coe]
834	  rw [cechCoeff_transport_eq_comparison s M r heqc hinvc,
835	    AwayComparison.comparison_comp_apply, AwayComparison.comparison_comp_apply,
836	    AwayComparison.comparison_comp_apply]
837	  · exact key
838	  · rw [CombinatorialCech.comp_succAbove_swap]; exact key
839	
840	/-- **Positive-degree exactness of the localised section {\v C}ech complex.**
841	After localising the standard-cover section {\v C}ech complex at a spanning element
842	`s r`, the resulting complex of away-localisations `A_σ = M_{s r · s_σ}` is exact in
843	positive degrees.  This is the dependent combinatorial core
844	`CombinatorialCech.depDiff_exact` fed the concrete coface `cechCoface`, prepend
845	`cechPrepend`, and the compatibilities `cech_hu`/`cech_hsh`/`cech_hcomm`.  It is the
846	`Function.Exact` input that `exact_of_isLocalized_span` consumes node by node in the
847	L1 categorical→module bridge (the remaining gaps: the finite-product localisation
848	`∏_σ M_{s_σ}` and the sheaf-section identification `F(D(s_σ)) = M_{s_σ}`). -/
849	lemma cechLocalized_exact (m : ℕ) :
850	    Function.Exact
851	      (CombinatorialCech.depDiff (A := fun _ σ => cechCoeff s M r σ) (cechCoface s M r)
852	        (m := m + 1))
853	      (CombinatorialCech.depDiff (A := fun _ σ => cechCoeff s M r σ) (cechCoface s M r)
854	        (m := m + 2)) :=
855	  CombinatorialCech.depDiff_exact (A := fun _ σ => cechCoeff s M r σ) r
856	    (cechCoface s M r) (cechPrepend s M r)
857	    (cech_hu s M r) (cech_hsh s M r) (cech_hcomm s M r) m
858	
859	end CechLocalized
860	
861	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the un-localised section Čech module complex `D•`
862	
863	`D^m` is the product `∏_{σ : Fin m → ι} M_{s_σ}` of away-localisations of
864	`M = Γ(Spec R, F)` at the multi-index products `s_σ = ∏ₖ s_{σ k}`; its differential
865	is the alternating sum of the canonical localisation comparison maps (index deletion
866	drops a factor, so `s_{σ∘dⱼ} ∣ s_σ`).  This is the concrete `R`-module complex `D•`
867	of `lem:section_cech_homology_exact` that the section Čech complex is identified
868	with.  Positive-degree exactness is reduced — via `exact_of_isLocalized_span` — to
869	the localised complexes already shown exact in `CechLocalized.cechLocalized_exact`;
870	the bridge is the localisation-transitivity lemma
871	`AwayComparison.comparison_isLocalizedModule` (applied here as
872	`dToCech_isLocalizedModule`). -/
873	
874	namespace SectionCechModule
875	
876	open CechLocalized AwayComparison
877	
878	variable {R : Type u} [CommRing R] {ι : Type*} (s : ι → R)
879	variable (M : Type u) [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M]
880	
881	/-- The un-localised section Čech coefficient `D_σ = M_{s_σ}`. -/
882	abbrev dCoeff {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin m → ι) : Type u :=
883	  LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (sprod s σ)) M
884	
885	/-- The un-localised coface comparison `δ : M_{s_{σ∘dⱼ}} → M_{s_σ}` (R-linear), the
886	canonical localisation map for the divisibility `s_{σ∘dⱼ} ∣ s_σ`. -/
887	noncomputable def dCoface (m : ℕ) (σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι) (j : Fin (m + 1)) :
888	    dCoeff s M (σ ∘ j.succAbove) →ₗ[R] dCoeff s M σ :=
889	  comparison
890	    (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (sprod s (σ ∘ j.succAbove))) M)
891	    (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (sprod s σ)) M)
892	    (Inverts.of_dvd (sprod_succAbove_dvd s σ j)
893	      (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (sprod s σ)) M))
894	
895	/-- The un-localised differential `d : D^m → D^{m+1}` as an `R`-linear map: the
896	alternating sum of cofaces, assembled with `LinearMap.pi` over the output tuple. -/
897	noncomputable def dDiff (m : ℕ) :
898	    (∀ σ : Fin m → ι, dCoeff s M σ) →ₗ[R] (∀ σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι, dCoeff s M σ) :=
899	  LinearMap.pi fun σ => ∑ j : Fin (m + 1),
900	    (-1 : ℤ) ^ (j : ℕ) • (dCoface s M m σ j ∘ₗ LinearMap.proj (σ ∘ j.succAbove))
901	
902	/-- Apply form of `dDiff`: the alternating sum of cofaces of the deleted faces. -/

2	\label{chap:Cohomology_CechHigherDirectImage}

6	% archon:covers AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/FreePresheafComplex.lean

8	% archon:covers AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/HigherDirectImagePresheaf.lean
9	% SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, \S{\v C}ech cohomology of
10	% quasi-coherent sheaves and \S Quasi-coherence of higher direct images
11	% (Lemmas \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial},
12	% \texttt{lemma-quasi-coherent-affine-cohomology-zero},
13	% \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent},

17	\section{Setup and motivation}
18	
19	This chapter constructs the higher derived direct images \(R^i f_*\mathcal{F}\) for
20	\(i \geq 1\) \emph{without appealing to injective resolutions in the category of
21	sheaves of modules}. The companion development of
22	Chapter~\ref{chap:Cohomology_HigherDirectImage} defines \(R^i f_*\) as a right
23	derived functor via injective resolutions, which requires the ambient category of
24	\(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules to have enough injectives. The {\v C}ech approach
25	developed here sidesteps this requirement:
26	it computes \(R^i f_*\mathcal{F}\) as the cohomology of an explicit complex built
27	from the pushforwards of \(\mathcal{F}\) over the finite intersections of an affine
28	open cover, and so produces an \emph{unconditional} construction of \(R^i f_*\) for
29	quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{F}\) and separated quasi-compact \(f\).
30	
31	Throughout, \(f : X \to S\) is a morphism of schemes that is quasi-compact and
32	separated (so that all finite intersections of an affine open cover of \(X\) are
33	again affine), and \(\mathcal{F}\) is a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module. We
34	write \(\operatorname{QCoh}(X)\) for the category of quasi-coherent
35	\(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules. A base change of \(f\) along a morphism \(g : S' \to S\)
36	is recorded by the cartesian square obtained from the fibre product
37	\(X' = X \times_S S'\):
38	\[
39	  \begin{array}{ccc}
40	    X' & \xrightarrow{\;g'\;} & X \\[2pt]
41	    {\scriptstyle f'}\big\downarrow & & \big\downarrow{\scriptstyle f} \\[2pt]
42	    S' & \xrightarrow{\;g\;} & S
43	  \end{array}
44	\]
45	Here \(f' : X' \to S'\) is the base change of \(f\), \(g' : X' \to X\) is the first
46	projection, and \(\mathcal{F}' = (g')^*\mathcal{F}\) is the pullback of
47	\(\mathcal{F}\) to \(X'\).
48	
49	\section{The {\v C}ech nerve and {\v C}ech complex of an affine cover}
50	
51	\begin{definition}
52	
53	\leanok
54	[{\v C}ech nerve of an affine open cover]
55	  \label{def:cech_nerve}
56	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.CechNerve}
57	  \uses{def:cover_cech_nerve, def:push_pull_functor, def:cech_nerve_cosimplicial, lem:push_pull_id, lem:push_pull_comp}
58	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, \S{\v C}ech cohomology of
59	  % quasi-coherent sheaves, opening paragraph and Lemma
60	  % \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial}
61	  % (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L36--67).
62	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $X$ be a scheme.
63	  % Let $U \subset X$ be an affine open.
64	  % Recall that a {\it standard open covering} of $U$ is a covering
65	  % of the form $\mathcal{U} : U = \bigcup_{i = 1}^n D(f_i)$
66	  % where $f_1, \ldots, f_n \in \Gamma(U, \mathcal{O}_X)$ generate
67	  % the unit ideal, see
68	  % Schemes, Definition \ref{schemes-definition-standard-covering}."
69	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, \S{\v C}ech cohomology of
70	  quasi-coherent sheaves.}
71	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and let
72	  \(\mathcal{U} : X = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) be a finite open cover of \(X\) by
73	  affine opens, with index set \(I\). For a finite nonempty tuple
74	  \(i_0, \ldots, i_p \in I\) write
75	  \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p} = U_{i_0} \cap \cdots \cap U_{i_p}\) for the corresponding
76	  intersection. The \emph{{\v C}ech nerve} of \(\mathcal{U}\) is the augmented
77	  \emph{cosimplicial} object in \(\operatorname{QCoh}(X)\) whose object in
78	  cosimplicial degree \(p\) is the direct image, along the open immersions
79	  \(j_{i_0 \ldots i_p} : U_{i_0 \ldots i_p} \hookrightarrow X\), of the restriction
80	  of \(\mathcal{O}_X\) (or of any chosen quasi-coherent sheaf) to the
81	  \((p+1)\)-fold intersections,
82	  \[
83	    [p] \;\longmapsto\;
84	      \prod_{(i_0, \ldots, i_p) \in I^{p+1}}
85	        (j_{i_0 \ldots i_p})_* \,\bigl(\,\cdot\,|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}}\bigr),
86	  \]
87	  with cofaces given by the restriction maps that omit one index and
88	  codegeneracies by the maps that repeat one index; the augmentation in degree
89	  \(-1\) is the object on all of \(X\). The variance is essential: the geometric
90	  nerve of the cover (the iterated fibre powers of \(\coprod_i U_i\) over \(X\),
91	  introduced in \S\ref{sec:cech_three_part} below) is a \emph{simplicial} object
92	  in \emph{schemes}, but applying the covariant direct-image functor turns it into
93	  a \emph{cosimplicial} object in \(\operatorname{QCoh}(X)\); consequently the
94	  associated alternating-coface complex is a \emph{cochain} complex (differentials
95	  raising degree), not a chain complex. When \(X\) is separated each intersection
96	  \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\) is itself affine. The local affine model is the
97	  \emph{standard open covering} \(U = \bigcup_{i=1}^n D(f_i)\) of an affine open
98	  \(U = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\) by basic opens, where \(f_1, \ldots, f_n \in A\)
99	  generate the unit ideal; on such a cover the intersections are again basic opens
100	  \(D(f_{i_0} \cdots f_{i_p})\).
101	\end{definition}
102	
103	\begin{definition}
104	
105	\leanok
106	[{\v C}ech complex of a quasi-coherent sheaf]
107	  \label{def:cech_complex}
108	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.CechComplex}
109	  \uses{def:cech_nerve, def:relative_cech_complex_of_nerve}
110	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma
111	  % \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (proof)
112	  % (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L59--77).
113	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Clearly the {\v C}ech complex
114	  % $\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})$
115	  % is identified with the complex
116	  % $$
117	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \to
118	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0i_1} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}} \to
119	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0i_1i_2} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}f_{i_2}} \to
120	  % \ldots
121	  % $$
122	  % We are asked to show that the extended complex
123	  % \begin{equation}
124	  % \label{equation-extended}
125	  % 0 \to
126	  % M \to
127	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \to
128	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0i_1} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}} \to
129	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0i_1i_2} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}f_{i_2}} \to
130	  % \ldots
131	  % \end{equation}
132	  % (whose truncation we have studied in
133	  % Algebra, Lemma \ref{algebra-lemma-cover-module}) is exact."
134	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
135	  \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (standard-cover {\v C}ech
136	  vanishing).}
137	  Let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module and let
138	  \(\mathcal{U} : X = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) be a finite affine open cover with
139	  all intersections \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\) affine (e.g.\ \(X\) separated). The
140	  \emph{{\v C}ech complex} \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})\)
141	  is the cochain complex obtained by applying the global-sections functor to the
142	  {\v C}ech nerve of Definition~\ref{def:cech_nerve} evaluated at \(\mathcal{F}\);
143	  in degree \(p\) it is
144	  \[
145	    \check{\mathcal{C}}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})
146	      = \prod_{(i_0, \ldots, i_p) \in I^{p+1}}
147	        \mathcal{F}\bigl(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\bigr),
148	  \]
149	  with differential \(d : \check{\mathcal{C}}^p \to \check{\mathcal{C}}^{p+1}\) the
150	  alternating sum \((d s)_{i_0 \ldots i_{p+1}} = \sum_{j=0}^{p+1} (-1)^j\,
151	  s_{i_0 \ldots \widehat{i_j} \ldots i_{p+1}}|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_{p+1}}}\) of the
152	  restriction maps. Over an affine \(U = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\) with
153	  \(\mathcal{F}|_U = \widetilde{M}\) and a standard cover by the
154	  \(D(f_i)\), this is the complex
155	  \(\prod_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \to \prod_{i_0 i_1} M_{f_{i_0} f_{i_1}} \to \cdots\) of
156	  localisations. The \emph{relative {\v C}ech complex} \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet
157	  (\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F})\) for \(f : X \to S\) is the analogous complex in
158	  \(\operatorname{QCoh}(S)\) obtained by applying \(f_*\) over each finite
159	  intersection instead of global sections; its terms are
160	  \(\prod_{i_0 \ldots i_p} (f|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}})_*
161	  \bigl(\mathcal{F}|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}}\bigr)\), and it is a complex in
162	  \(\operatorname{QCoh}(S)\) because each \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\) is affine and the
163	  pushforward of a quasi-coherent sheaf along a quasi-compact quasi-separated
164	  morphism is quasi-coherent.
165	\end{definition}
166	
167	\subsection{The three-part construction of the relative {\v C}ech complex}
168	\label{sec:cech_three_part}
169	
170	It is convenient to factor the construction of
171	Definitions~\ref{def:cech_nerve}--\ref{def:cech_complex} into three independent
172	pieces. Two of them are purely formal and unconditional; the sole non-trivial
173	mathematical content is isolated in the middle piece.
174	
175	\paragraph{(1) Geometric backbone — the augmented {\v C}ech nerve of an arrow.}
176	Package the affine cover as a single morphism
177	\(\coprod_{i \in I} U_i \longrightarrow X\) (the canonical map out of the
178	coproduct induced by the inclusions \(U_i \hookrightarrow X\)). The augmented
179	{\v C}ech nerve of this arrow is an augmented \emph{simplicial scheme} over
180	\(X\): its object in simplicial degree \(p\) is the \((p+1)\)-fold fibre power
181	\[
182	  \Bigl(\coprod_{i} U_i\Bigr)^{\times_X (p+1)}
183	    \;=\;
184	    \coprod_{(i_0, \ldots, i_p) \in I^{p+1}}
185	      U_{i_0} \times_X \cdots \times_X U_{i_p},
186	\]
187	with the augmentation the cover map to \(X\). Because the \(U_i\) are open
188	subschemes of \(X\), each fibre product \(U_{i_0} \times_X \cdots \times_X U_{i_p}\)
189	is canonically the intersection \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\). This backbone exists
190	\emph{unconditionally}: it uses only that the category of schemes has coproducts
191	and finite limits (hence the wide pullbacks underlying the {\v C}ech nerve of an
192	arrow). It carries no sheaf-theoretic data yet — it is the simplicial diagram of
193	spaces over which the construction is indexed.
194	
195	\paragraph{(2) Push--pull functor — lifting the backbone to modules.}
196	The geometric backbone is turned into a cosimplicial \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module by
197	the \emph{relative-direct-image functor on the over-category}
198	\[
199	  G : (X/\mathbf{Sch})^{\mathrm{op}} \longrightarrow \operatorname{QCoh}(X),
200	  \qquad
201	  (Y \xrightarrow{\,p\,} X) \;\longmapsto\; p_*\, p^*\mathcal{F},
202	\]
203	sending an \(X\)-scheme \(p : Y \to X\) to the pushforward along \(p\) of the
204	pullback \(p^*\mathcal{F}\). Composing \(G\) with the (opposite of the) simplicial
205	backbone of (1) produces exactly the augmented cosimplicial object of
206	Definition~\ref{def:cech_nerve}: on the degree-\(p\) piece
207	\(p : U_{i_0 \ldots i_p} \hookrightarrow X\) one has
208	\(p_* p^*\mathcal{F} = (j_{i_0 \ldots i_p})_*(\mathcal{F}|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}})\),
209	and a morphism of \(X\)-schemes \(h : (Y_1, p_1) \to (Y_2, p_2)\) (so
210	\(h \circ p_2 = p_1\)\,, reading \(p\) as the structure map) induces the
211	restriction comparison \(G(p_2) \to G(p_1)\) via the unit/counit of the
212	pull-push adjunction together with the over-triangle identifying the two
213	structure maps. This functor \(G\) is the \emph{one non-formal step} in the whole
214	construction. Its functoriality --- that \(G\) respects identities and composition
215	of morphisms over \(X\) --- is precisely a statement about the coherence of the
216	comparison isomorphisms \((p \circ q)_* \cong p_* q_*\) and
217	\((p \circ q)^* \cong q^* p^*\) for pushforward and pullback of sheaves of
218	modules, together with their compatibility with the adjunction units. Concretely,
219	pullback and pushforward of sheaves of modules are organised as a
220	pseudofunctor \(\mathrm{LocallyDiscrete}(\mathbf{Sch}^{\mathrm{op}}) \to
221	\mathbf{Adj}\,\mathbf{Cat}\) (sending a morphism to the adjoint pair
222	\(p^* \dashv p_*\)), and the comparison isomorphisms
223	\((p \circ q)^* \cong q^* p^*\), \((p \circ q)_* \cong p_* q_*\) are its structure
224	\(2\)-cells. The functoriality of \(G\) needs exactly three facts: the mate identity
225	expressing the conjugate of the inverse pullback comparison as the pushforward
226	comparison, its normalisation at the identity morphism, and the pseudofunctor
227	unitor and pentagon (associativity) coherences. The laws are nonetheless not formal
228	trivialities: each is a multi-step mate calculation that transports the comparison
229	isomorphisms along the over-triangle \(g \circ p_2 = p_1\) of structure maps and
230	past the adjunction unit, transporting along the over-triangle equality; the
231	obstacle is the bookkeeping of those transports, not a missing geometric ingredient.
232	
233	\paragraph{(3) Coherence-free plumbing — from the nerve to the cochain complex.}
234	The passage from the augmented cosimplicial \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module of (1)--(2)
235	to the relative {\v C}ech complex in \(\operatorname{QCoh}(S)\) is again purely
236	formal and uses \emph{no} pushforward/pullback coherence. One forgets the
237	augmentation, pushes the cosimplicial object forward along \(f : X \to S\) (a
238	covariant operation applied degreewise), and takes the alternating-coface-map
239	cochain complex of the resulting cosimplicial object of \(\mathcal{O}_S\)-modules.
240	This step relies only on the preadditivity of \(\operatorname{QCoh}(S)\): the
241	alternating sum \(\sum_{j} (-1)^j d^j\) of the cofaces is defined in any
242	preadditive category and squares to zero by the cosimplicial identities. Thus the
243	relative {\v C}ech complex \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F})\)
244	of Definition~\ref{def:cech_complex} is genuinely \emph{defined} in terms of the
245	{\v C}ech nerve of Definition~\ref{def:cech_nerve}: once the nerve is constructed,
246	the complex follows with no further input. In summary, of the three pieces only
247	the push--pull functor (2) requires content beyond formal category theory, and
248	that content is the pseudofunctor coherence of pushforward and pullback: the
249	comparison \(2\)-cells of the pseudofunctor
250	\(\mathrm{LocallyDiscrete}(\mathbf{Sch}^{\mathrm{op}}) \to \mathbf{Adj}\,\mathbf{Cat}\)
251	and their unitor/pentagon identities.
252	
253	\paragraph{Formal bricks of the three-part construction.}
254	The geometric backbone (1), the push--pull functor (2), and the plumbing (3) are
255	recorded as the following declarations. The backbone and plumbing are
256	unconditional; the push--pull object and morphism maps are likewise unconditional
257	bricks, and the functor laws assembling them are the lone coherence statement.
258	
259	\begin{definition}\leanok
260	[Arrow of an affine cover]
261	  \label{def:cover_arrow}
262	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.coverArrow}
263	  Let \(\mathcal{U} : X = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) be an affine open cover. Its
264	  \emph{cover arrow} is the single morphism
265	  \(\coprod_{i \in I} U_i \longrightarrow X\) obtained as the universal map out of
266	  the coproduct induced by the open immersions \(U_i \hookrightarrow X\). Packaging
267	  the cover as one arrow lets the generic {\v C}ech-nerve-of-an-arrow machinery
268	  apply.
269	\end{definition}
270	
271	\begin{definition}[Augmented {\v C}ech nerve of a cover]
272	  \label{def:cover_cech_nerve}
273	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.coverCechNerve, AlgebraicGeometry.coverCechNerveOver, AlgebraicGeometry.coverCechNerveOverAug}
274	  \uses{def:cover_arrow}
275	  The augmented {\v C}ech nerve of the cover arrow of
276	  Definition~\ref{def:cover_arrow}: the augmented \emph{simplicial scheme} over
277	  \(X\) whose degree-\(p\) object is the \((p+1)\)-fold fibre power
278	  \[
279	    \Bigl(\coprod_{i} U_i\Bigr)^{\times_X (p+1)}
280	      = \coprod_{(i_0, \ldots, i_p) \in I^{p+1}}
281	        U_{i_0} \times_X \cdots \times_X U_{i_p},
282	  \]
283	  with augmentation the cover map to \(X\). It exists unconditionally because the
284	  category of schemes has all finite limits (hence the wide pullbacks underlying the
285	  {\v C}ech nerve of an arrow). This is the geometric backbone (1).
286	\end{definition}
287	
288	\begin{definition}\leanok
289	[Push--pull object map]
290	  \label{def:push_pull_obj}
291	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pushPullObj}
292	  The object map of the push--pull functor \(G\): to an \(X\)-scheme
293	  \(p : Y \to X\) it assigns
294	  \[
295	    G(Y, p) \;=\; p_*\, p^*\mathcal{F},
296	  \]
297	  the pushforward along \(p\) of the pullback \(p^*\mathcal{F}\).
298	\end{definition}
299	
300	\begin{definition}[Push--pull comparison map]
301	  \label{def:push_pull_map}
302	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pushPullMap, AlgebraicGeometry.rawPushPullMap, AlgebraicGeometry.pushPullMap_eq_raw, AlgebraicGeometry.rawPushPullMap_self, AlgebraicGeometry.rawPushPullMap_self_gen, AlgebraicGeometry.pushforwardComp_hom_app_id, AlgebraicGeometry.pushPull_unit_comp}
303	  \uses{def:push_pull_obj}
304	  The morphism map of \(G\). A morphism \(g : (Y_2, p_2) \to (Y_1, p_1)\) of
305	  \(X\)-schemes has underlying map \(\bar g : Y_2 \to Y_1\) satisfying the
306	  over-triangle \(p_1 \circ \bar g = p_2\); the contravariant functor produces the
307	  restriction comparison \(G(Y_1, p_1) \to G(Y_2, p_2)\) as the five-step composite
308	  \[
309	    p_{1*}\,p_1^*\mathcal{F}
310	      \xrightarrow{\,p_{1*}(\eta)\,}
311	    p_{1*}\,\bar g_*\,\bar g^*\,p_1^*\mathcal{F}
312	      \xrightarrow{\,\sim\,}
313	    (p_1 \circ \bar g)_*\,\bar g^*\,p_1^*\mathcal{F}
314	      \;=\;
315	    p_{2*}\,\bar g^*\,p_1^*\mathcal{F}
316	      \xrightarrow{\,p_{2*}(\sim)\,}
317	    p_{2*}\,p_2^*\mathcal{F},
318	  \]
319	  where \(\eta\) is the unit of the adjunction \(\bar g^* \dashv \bar g_*\), the
320	  second arrow is the pushforward comparison \(p_{1*}\bar g_* \cong (p_1 \bar g)_*\),
321	  the equality is the substitution \(p_2 = p_1 \circ \bar g\) of the over-triangle,
322	  and the last arrow applies \(p_{2*}\) to the pullback comparison
323	  \(\bar g^* p_1^* \cong (p_1 \bar g)^* = p_2^*\). The two over-triangle
324	  substitutions are realised as coercions along \(p_1 \circ \bar g = p_2\).
325	\end{definition}
326	
327	\begin{lemma}
328	[Push--pull functor — identity law]
329	  \label{lem:push_pull_id}
330	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pushPullMap_id}
331	  \uses{def:push_pull_obj, def:push_pull_map}
332	  The morphism map of Definition~\ref{def:push_pull_map} respects identities:
333	  \(G(\mathrm{id}_Y) = \mathrm{id}_{G(Y)}\) for every \(X\)-scheme \(Y\).
334	\end{lemma}
335	
336	\begin{proof}
337	  A direct calculation with comparison isomorphisms, with no sectionwise content.
338	  Rewrite the head \(p_*(\eta)\) followed by the pushforward comparison as the
339	  conjugate-mate of the inverse pullback comparison; the identity leg then collapses
340	  by the identity normalisation of the pseudofunctor together with right unitality,
341	  and the two over-triangle transport coercions cancel against the unitality coercion.
342	\end{proof}
343	
344	\begin{lemma}
345	[Push--pull functor — composition law]
346	  \label{lem:push_pull_comp}
347	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pushPullMap_comp, AlgebraicGeometry.rawPushPullMap_comp, AlgebraicGeometry.pushPull_pentagon}
348	  \uses{def:push_pull_obj, def:push_pull_map, lem:push_pull_unit_mate, lem:push_pull_transport_cancel}
349	  The morphism map of Definition~\ref{def:push_pull_map} is contravariantly functorial:
350	  for composable morphisms \(h : Y_3 \to Y_2\), \(g : Y_2 \to Y_1\) of \(X\)-schemes,
351	  \[
352	    G(g \circ h) = G(h) \circ G(g).
353	  \]
354	  Together with Lemma~\ref{lem:push_pull_id} this assembles
355	  Definitions~\ref{def:push_pull_obj}--\ref{def:push_pull_map} into a functor
356	  \(G : (X/\mathbf{Sch})^{\mathrm{op}} \to \operatorname{QCoh}(X)\); composing \(G\) with
357	  the backbone of Definition~\ref{def:cover_cech_nerve} yields the {\v C}ech nerve of
358	  Definition~\ref{def:cech_nerve}.
359	\end{lemma}
360	
361	\begin{proof}
362	  \uses{lem:push_pull_unit_mate, lem:push_pull_transport_cancel}
363	  The composition identity \(G(g \circ h) = G(h) \circ G(g)\) follows from three
364	  coherence facts. First, the composite adjunction unit \(\eta^{g \circ h}\) decomposes into
365	  the iterated units \(\eta^{g}\), \(\eta^{h}\) via the mate identity of
366	  Lemma~\ref{lem:push_pull_unit_mate} and the naturality of the adjunction unit. Second,
367	  the resulting pullback-side identity is the \emph{pentagon coherence} of the pullback
368	  pseudofunctor — the associativity \(2\)-cell relating the two bracketings of the comparison
369	  isomorphism \((p \circ q \circ r)^* \cong r^* q^* p^*\). Third, the over-triangle transport
370	  coherences are absorbed by Lemma~\ref{lem:push_pull_transport_cancel}.
371	\end{proof}
372	
373	\begin{lemma}
374	[Base-change unit (mate) identity for the push--pull head]
375	  \label{lem:push_pull_unit_mate}
376	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pushPull_unit_mate}
377	  \uses{def:push_pull_map}
378	  For composable scheme morphisms \(f : A \to B\), \(p : B \to Z\) and a
379	  \(\mathcal{O}_Z\)-module \(N\), the adjunction unit \(\eta^p_N\) of
380	  \(p^* \dashv p_*\) at \(N\), followed by \(p_*\) of the unit \(\eta^f\) for
381	  \(f^* \dashv f_*\) at \(p^*N\), followed by the pushforward comparison
382	  \(p_* f_* \cong (f \circ p)_*\), equals the unit \(\eta^{f \circ p}_N\) for the
383	  composite followed by \((f \circ p)_*\) applied to the inverse pullback
384	  comparison \((f \circ p)^* \cong f^* p^*\):
385	  \[
386	    \eta^p_N \;\circ\; p_*(\eta^f_{p^*N}) \;\circ\;
387	      \bigl(p_* f_* \cong (f \circ p)_*\bigr)
388	    \;=\;
389	    \eta^{f \circ p}_N \;\circ\; (f \circ p)_*\bigl((f\circ p)^* \cong f^* p^*\bigr)^{-1}.
390	  \]
391	  This is the mate-calculus core that converts the single-morphism unit
392	  \(\eta^{f \circ p}\) into the iterated units \(\eta^p, \eta^f\); it is the
393	  reusable ingredient consumed by the functoriality (pentagon) law of the
394	  push--pull comparison map of Definition~\ref{def:push_pull_map}.
395	\end{lemma}
396	
397	\begin{proof}
398	  A direct mate-calculus computation: unfold both sides using the definition of
399	  the adjunction unit for the composite and the pseudofunctor structure maps, then
400	  verify the equality by the mate identity for the pushforward/pullback comparison.
401	\end{proof}
402	
403	\begin{lemma}
404	[Over-triangle transport cancellation for the push--pull tail]
405	  \label{lem:push_pull_transport_cancel}
406	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pushPull_transport_cancel}
407	  \uses{def:push_pull_map}
408	  Let \(\bar g : Y_2 \to Y_1\), \(p_1 : Y_1 \to X\), \(p_2 : Y_2 \to X\) satisfy the
409	  over-triangle \(\bar g \circ p_1 = p_2\), and let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a
410	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module. Then the pullback-comparison leg of the push--pull map,
411	  conjugated by the two \(\mathrm{eqToHom}\) coercions along the over-triangle,
412	  collapses to the transport-light form
413	  \[
414	    e_1 \;\circ\; p_{2*}\bigl((\bar g^* p_1^* \cong (\bar g p_1)^*)_{\mathcal{F}}\bigr)
415	      \;\circ\; e_2
416	    \;=\;
417	    (\bar g p_1)_*\bigl((\bar g^* p_1^* \cong (\bar g p_1)^*)_{\mathcal{F}}\bigr)
418	      \;\circ\; e_3,
419	  \]
420	  where \(e_1, e_2, e_3\) are the \(\mathrm{eqToHom}\) coercions induced by
421	  \(\bar g \circ p_1 = p_2\). It states the over-triangle cancellation in the
422	  push--pull comparison map of Definition~\ref{def:push_pull_map} with the
423	  over-triangle equality as a free hypothesis, so a single substitution turns the
424	  transports into identities; it is the reusable pre-coherence brick for the
425	  composition (pentagon) law.
426	\end{lemma}
427	
428	\begin{proof}
429	  Substitute the over-triangle equality \(\bar g \circ p_1 = p_2\) to replace
430	  transport coercions by identities, then verify the cancellation by direct
431	  calculation with the pushforward comparison.
432	\end{proof}

434	\begin{definition}

439	  The \emph{push--pull functor}
440	  \[
441	    G : (X/\mathbf{Sch})^{\mathrm{op}} \longrightarrow \operatorname{QCoh}(X),
442	  \]
443	  assembling the object map \(p \mapsto p_*\, p^*\mathcal{F}\) of
444	  Definition~\ref{def:push_pull_obj} and the comparison morphism map of
445	  Definition~\ref{def:push_pull_map} into a genuine functor. Functoriality is exactly the two
446	  laws established above: the identity law
447	  Lemma~\ref{lem:push_pull_id} and the composition law Lemma~\ref{lem:push_pull_comp}. The
448	  functor is contravariant in the over-category \((X/\mathbf{Sch})\); it is the lift of the
449	  geometric backbone (1) to \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules described as step (2) above.
450	\end{definition}

452	\begin{definition}
453	[{\v C}ech nerve cosimplicial object]
454	  \label{def:cech_nerve_cosimplicial}

456	  \uses{def:cover_cech_nerve, def:push_pull_functor}
457	  The (non-augmented) cosimplicial \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module obtained by transporting the
458	  geometric {\v C}ech backbone in \((X/\mathbf{Sch})\) of
459	  Definition~\ref{def:cover_cech_nerve} through the push--pull functor
460	  \(G\) of Definition~\ref{def:push_pull_functor}: degreewise, the \(p\)-th term is the
461	  product over \((i_0, \ldots, i_p) \in I^{p+1}\) of the \((p+1)\)-fold-intersection
462	  pushforwards \((j_{i_0 \ldots i_p})_*(\mathcal{F}|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}})\). This is the
463	  underlying cosimplicial object that the {\v C}ech nerve of
464	  Definition~\ref{def:cech_nerve} augments by the structure map \(\mathcal{F} \to
465	  \mathcal{C}^0\).

471	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.relativeCechComplexOfNerve}
472	  \uses{def:cech_nerve}

474	  \(N\), the \emph{relative {\v C}ech cochain complex} in \(\operatorname{QCoh}(S)\)
475	  obtained by forgetting the augmentation of \(N\), pushing the resulting
476	  cosimplicial object forward degreewise along \(f\), and taking the
477	  alternating-coface-map cochain complex. This passage uses only the preadditivity

479	  the coherence-free plumbing (3) that turns the nerve of
480	  Definition~\ref{def:cech_nerve} into the {\v C}ech complex.
481	\end{definition}

483	\section{Affine acyclicity of the {\v C}ech complex}
484	
485	\begin{definition}
486	[Standard affine cover from a spanning family]
487	  \label{def:standard_affine_cover}
488	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.affineOpenCoverOfSpanRangeEqTop}
489	  \mathlibok
490	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib (\texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Cover.Open}).}
491	  Let \(R\) be a commutative ring (so \(R = \Gamma(\operatorname{Spec} R,
492	  \mathcal{O})\)) and let \(s : \iota \to R\) be a family of elements that generate
493	  the unit ideal, \(\operatorname{Ideal.span}(\operatorname{range} s) = \top\). The
494	  \emph{standard affine open cover} of \(\operatorname{Spec} R\) associated with
495	  \(s\) is the affine open cover whose index set is \(\iota\), whose \(i\)-th member
496	  is the basic open \(D(s_i)\), and whose \(i\)-th structure morphism is the map of
497	  affine schemes induced by the localisation homomorphism \(R \to R_{s_i}\); that
498	  is, in Mathlib notation the defining equation
499	  \(\bigl(\text{affineOpenCoverOfSpanRangeEqTop}\bigr)_f\, i =
500	  \operatorname{Spec.map}\bigl(\text{algebraMap}\; R\; (\operatorname{Localization.Away}(s_i))\bigr)\),
501	  so that the sections over the \(i\)-th piece are the \emph{away} localisation
502	  \(R_{s_i} = \operatorname{Localization.Away}(s_i)\) and the geometric piece is
503	  \(D(s_i)\). This is exactly the standard open covering
504	  \(\operatorname{Spec} R = \bigcup_i D(s_i)\) by basic opens, packaged as a single
505	  cover object. It is the spanning-family bundle \((s, hs)\) that the affine
506	  {\v C}ech vanishing of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine} is stated over.
507	\end{definition}
508	
509	\subsection{The categorical--module bridge (L1)}
510	\label{sec:cech_l1_bridge}
511	
512	The affine vanishing of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine} is a statement about the
513	\emph{section} {\v C}ech complex \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U},
514	\mathcal{F})\) of Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_complex} --- a cochain complex of
515	abelian groups --- whereas the combinatorial contracting-homotopy core operates on a
516	concrete complex of localised \(R\)-modules. The passage between the two, the
517	\emph{categorical--module bridge}, decomposes into two self-contained statements: the
518	section identification (each section group is an away localisation, with the
519	restriction maps the canonical localisation maps) and the homology-to-exactness
520	reduction (vanishing of the abstract homology is equivalent to exactness of the
521	underlying localised-module complex). These are isolated as
522	Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized} and
523	Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_homology_exact} below; the assembly --- feeding each
524	positive-degree node through the local-to-global exactness criterion and closing with
525	the dependent combinatorial port --- is carried out in the proof of
526	Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine}.
527	
528	\begin{definition}
529	
530	[Quasi-coherent sections over a basic open are an away localisation]
531	  \label{def:qcoh_sections_localized}
532	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.qcohSectionsAwayLocalized,
533	    AlgebraicGeometry.basicOpen_sprod,
534	    AlgebraicGeometry.iInf_fin_succ,
535	    AlgebraicGeometry.qcohRestriction_eq_comparison}
536	  \uses{def:section_cech_complex, def:standard_affine_cover}
537	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Schemes, Lemma \texttt{lemma-spec-sheaves} (Tag 01HV),
538	  % items (4)--(5) (read from references/stacks-schemes.tex, L701--707).
539	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "(4) For every $f\in R$ we have $\Gamma(D(f), \widetilde M) = M_f$
540	  % as an $R_f$-module.
541	  % (5) Whenever $D(g) \subset D(f)$ the restriction mappings
542	  % on $\mathcal{O}_{\Spec(R)}$ and $\widetilde M$
543	  % are the maps
544	  % $R_f \to R_g$ and $M_f \to M_g$ from Lemma
545	  % \ref{lemma-standard-open}."
546	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Schemes, \texttt{lemma-spec-sheaves} (Tag 01HV),
547	  items (4)--(5).}
548	  Let \(R\) be a commutative ring, \(\mathcal{F}\) a quasi-coherent
549	  \(\mathcal{O}_{\operatorname{Spec} R}\)-module, and write
550	  \(M = \Gamma(\operatorname{Spec} R, \mathcal{F})\) for its module of global
551	  sections. For every \(g \in R\), the restriction-of-sections map
552	  \(M = \mathcal{F}(\operatorname{Spec} R) \to \mathcal{F}(D(g))\) exhibits the
553	  section group \(\mathcal{F}(D(g))\) as the away localisation \(M_g\) of \(M\); that
554	  is, this map is an \(\operatorname{IsLocalizedModule}\) for the multiplicative
555	  submonoid \(\operatorname{Submonoid.powers} g\) of powers of \(g\). Moreover, for
556	  \(D(g_2) \subseteq D(g_1)\) the restriction map
557	  \(\mathcal{F}(D(g_1)) \to \mathcal{F}(D(g_2))\) is, under these identifications, the
558	  canonical localisation map \(M_{g_1} \to M_{g_2}\) (the unique \(R\)-linear map
559	  compatible with the localisation units). Item~(4) of Tag~01HV gives the first
560	  assertion for \(\mathcal{F} = \widetilde{M}\) (\(\Gamma(D(g), \widetilde{M}) = M_g\)
561	  as an \(R_g\)-module) and item~(5) gives the second (the restriction along
562	  \(D(g) \subset D(f)\) is the localisation map \(M_f \to M_g\)).
563	
564	  The module-level input on the side of Mathlib is the development
565	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Tilde}: the comparison map
566	  \((\operatorname{tilde.toOpen} M\, (D(g))).\mathrm{hom}\) carries the instance
567	  \(\operatorname{IsLocalizedModule}(\operatorname{Submonoid.powers} g)\), and
568	  \(\operatorname{isUnit\_algebraMap\_end\_basicOpen}\) records that \(g\) acts
569	  invertibly on the sections over \(D(g)\); the restriction compatibility is
570	  \(\operatorname{tilde.toOpen\_res}\). These supply the statement \emph{verbatim} for
571	  the standard sheaf \(\widetilde{M}\). For an \emph{arbitrary} quasi-coherent
572	  \(\mathcal{F}\) the remaining step is the canonical comparison
573	  \(\mathcal{F} \cong \widetilde{\Gamma(\operatorname{Spec} R, \mathcal{F})}\), i.e.\
574	  that the counit \(\operatorname{Scheme.Modules.fromTildeΓ}\) is an isomorphism. This
575	  is \emph{not} yet available in Mathlib for a general quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{F}\):
576	  Mathlib provides it only from a \emph{global} presentation
577	  (\(\operatorname{isIso\_fromTildeΓ\_of\_presentation}\)), whereas quasi-coherence
578	  supplies only \emph{local} presentation data on a cover. Globalising the local data
579	  on the affine \(\operatorname{Spec} R\) --- the affine equivalence
580	  \(\operatorname{QCoh}(\operatorname{Spec} R) \simeq \operatorname{Mod}(R)\), Stacks
581	  Tag~01I8 --- is the one genuine gap this definition records as a build target; it is
582	  the canonical missing affine equivalence and is self-contained.
583	
584	  Two combinatorial--geometric ingredients package the identification into the form
585	  consumed by Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_homology_exact}. First, the multi-index
586	  intersection identity
587	  \(\bigl(\bigcap_k D(s_{\sigma(k)})\bigr) = D\bigl(\prod_k s_{\sigma(k)}\bigr)\) in
588	  \((\operatorname{Spec} R).\mathrm{Opens}\) (\(\operatorname{basicOpen\_sprod}\), proved
589	  by induction on the index length using the infimum-splitting helper
590	  \(\operatorname{iInf\_fin\_succ}\) and \(\operatorname{PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen\_mul}\)):
591	  it rewrites the \((p+1)\)-fold intersection over a multi-index \(\sigma\) as the single
592	  basic open \(D(s_\sigma)\) of the product \(s_\sigma = \prod_k s_{\sigma(k)}\), so the
593	  section group there is the away localisation \(M_{s_\sigma}\). Second, the restriction
594	  compatibility of item~(5) is made explicit: for a basic-open inclusion
595	  \(D(b) \subseteq D(a)\) the presheaf restriction map of \(\widetilde{M}\) between the
596	  two section groups \emph{is} the away-localisation comparison
597	  \(\operatorname{AwayComparison.comparison}\) of the two localisation units
598	  (\(\operatorname{qcohRestriction\_eq\_comparison}\), by the uniqueness
599	  \(\operatorname{AwayComparison.comparison\_unique}\) of a map of localised modules);
600	  this is the single differential brick that the {\v C}ech coface match of
601	  Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_homology_exact} feeds on.
602	\end{definition}
