604	\begin{lemma}
605	[Section {\v C}ech homology vanishes iff the localised complex is exact]
606	  \label{lem:section_cech_homology_exact}
607	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCech_homology_exact,
608	    AlgebraicGeometry.AwayComparison.Inverts,
609	    AlgebraicGeometry.AwayComparison.Inverts.isUnit_powers,
610	    AlgebraicGeometry.AwayComparison.Inverts.mul,
611	    AlgebraicGeometry.AwayComparison.Inverts.of_dvd,
612	    AlgebraicGeometry.AwayComparison.comparison,
613	    AlgebraicGeometry.AwayComparison.comparison_apply,
614	    AlgebraicGeometry.AwayComparison.comparison_comp,
615	    AlgebraicGeometry.AwayComparison.comparison_comp_apply,
616	    AlgebraicGeometry.AwayComparison.comparison_comp_structure,
617	    AlgebraicGeometry.AwayComparison.comparison_self,
618	    AlgebraicGeometry.AwayComparison.comparison_unique,
619	    AlgebraicGeometry.CechLocalized.cechCoeff,
620	    AlgebraicGeometry.CechLocalized.cechCoeff_transport_eq_comparison,
621	    AlgebraicGeometry.CechLocalized.cechCoface,
622	    AlgebraicGeometry.CechLocalized.cechLocalized_exact,
623	    AlgebraicGeometry.CechLocalized.cechPrepend,
624	    AlgebraicGeometry.CechLocalized.cech_hcomm,
625	    AlgebraicGeometry.CechLocalized.cech_hsh,
626	    AlgebraicGeometry.CechLocalized.cech_hu,
627	    AlgebraicGeometry.CechLocalized.sprod,
628	    AlgebraicGeometry.CechLocalized.sprod_cons,
629	    AlgebraicGeometry.CechLocalized.sprod_succAbove_dvd}
630	  \uses{def:section_cech_complex, def:qcoh_sections_localized, lem:section_cech_module_exact,
631	    lem:section_cech_product_equiv, lem:section_cech_objd_apply,
632	    lem:section_cech_coface_match, lem:section_cech_ab_exact}
633	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma
634	  % \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (the {\v C}ech complex is
635	  % the complex of localisations) (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex,
636	  % L58--67).
637	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Clearly the {\v C}ech complex
638	  % $\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})$
639	  % is identified with the complex
640	  % $$
641	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \to
642	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0i_1} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}} \to
643	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0i_1i_2} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}f_{i_2}} \to
644	  % \ldots
645	  % $$"
646	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
647	  \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (identification with the
648	  complex of localisations).}
649	  Let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_{\operatorname{Spec}
650	  R}\)-module and \(\mathcal{U}\) the standard cover associated with a spanning family
651	  \(s : \iota \to R\). Under the section identification of
652	  Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized}, the section {\v C}ech complex
653	  \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})\) of
654	  Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_complex} is isomorphic, as a cochain complex, to
655	  the concrete complex of localised \(R\)-modules
656	  \[
657	    D^\bullet : \quad
658	    \prod_{\sigma : \{0\} \to \iota} M_{s_\sigma} \;\longrightarrow\;
659	    \prod_{\sigma : \{0,1\} \to \iota} M_{s_\sigma} \;\longrightarrow\;
660	    \cdots,
661	    \qquad
662	    s_\sigma = \prod_{k} s_{\sigma(k)},
663	  \]
664	  whose differential is the alternating sum of the canonical localisation maps. The
665	  intended top-level conclusion is the directed homology vanishing
666	  \[
667	    1 \leq p
668	    \;\Longrightarrow\;
669	    \operatorname{IsZero}\bigl(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U},
670	      \mathcal{F}).\mathrm{homology}\, p\bigr),
671	  \]
672	  which is the form the affine vanishing
673	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine} (\(\operatorname{sectionCech\_affine\_vanishing}\))
674	  consumes; it is read off from positive-degree exactness of the underlying group
675	  homomorphisms,
676	  \(\operatorname{Function.Exact}\bigl(D^{p-1} \to D^p \to D^{p+1}\bigr)\), which is
677	  Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_module_exact}.
678	
679	  The reduction from the abstract homology to this group-level exactness is \emph{not}
680	  a \(\operatorname{ModuleCat}\) statement: the degree-\(p\) object of
681	  \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})\) is the \emph{categorical
682	  product}
683	  \(\prod^{c}_{\sigma : \mathrm{Fin}(p+1) \to \iota}
684	  \mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))\) in the category \(\mathrm{Ab}\) of abelian groups (the
685	  alternating-coface-map complex of the \(\mathrm{Ab}\)-valued cosimplicial object
686	  \(\operatorname{sectionCechCosimplicial}\)), not an \(R\)-module object, so the
687	  module-category criterion \(\operatorname{ShortComplex.moduleCat\_exact\_iff}\) does
688	  not apply. The correct chain runs through three steps, isolated as the sub-lemmas
689	  below: \(\operatorname{IsZero}\) of the homology is \(\operatorname{ExactAt}\) at
690	  \(p\) (\(\operatorname{exactAt\_iff\_isZero\_homology}\)); \(\operatorname{ExactAt}\)
691	  of a short complex in \(\mathrm{Ab}\) is the underlying-group exactness
692	  \(\forall x_2,\; g(x_2) = 0 \Rightarrow \exists x_1,\; f(x_1) = x_2\) via the abelian
693	  analogue \(\operatorname{ShortComplex.ab\_exact\_iff}\)
694	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_ab_exact}); and the categorical product is moved to the
695	  per-\(\sigma\) localised modules by the element-level product equivalence
696	  \(\operatorname{ToType}(\prod^{c} F_\sigma) \simeq \prod_\sigma
697	  \operatorname{ToType}(F_\sigma)\)
698	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_product_equiv}), under which the {\v C}ech coface
699	  differential matches the localisation differential \(\operatorname{dDiff}\) of
700	  \(D^\bullet\) (the abstract cosimplicial unfold
701	  Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_objd_apply} followed by the tilde-bridge identification
702	  Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_coface_match}). Transporting the
703	  already-proved exactness \(\operatorname{dDiff\_exact}\) of
704	  Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_module_exact} across the product equivalence then
705	  discharges the group-level exactness. The active target is the tilde case
706	  \(\mathcal{F} = \widetilde{M}\), for which the per-coordinate comparisons are
707	  immediate from \(\operatorname{qcohRestriction\_eq\_comparison}\); a general
708	  quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{F}\) is reduced to this case via the affine equivalence
709	  \(\mathcal{F} \cong \widetilde{\Gamma\mathcal{F}}\) (Stacks Tag~01I8), the
710	  separately-deferred globalisation gap recorded in
711	  Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized}.
712	\end{lemma}
713	
714	\begin{proof}
715	  \uses{def:qcoh_sections_localized, def:section_cech_complex}
716	  The term identification is the degreewise statement of
717	  Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized}: the degree-\(p\) section group
718	  \(\prod_\sigma \mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))\) is \(\prod_\sigma M_{s_\sigma}\), since
719	  each \((p+1)\)-fold intersection \(D(s_{\sigma(0)}) \cap \cdots \cap
720	  D(s_{\sigma(p)})\) is the basic open \(D(s_\sigma)\) of the product
721	  \(s_\sigma = \prod_k s_{\sigma(k)}\) and the sections there are the away
722	  localisation \(M_{s_\sigma}\). The differential identification is item~(5) of
723	  Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized}: each face restriction
724	  \(\mathcal{F}(D(s_{\sigma \circ d_j})) \to \mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))\) is the
725	  canonical localisation map \(M_{s_{\sigma \circ d_j}} \to M_{s_\sigma}\), so the
726	  alternating sum of restrictions is the alternating sum of localisation maps, the
727	  differential of \(D^\bullet\) (the coface match of
728	  Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_coface_match}). The homology vanishing then follows in
729	  three steps. First, \(\operatorname{exactAt\_iff\_isZero\_homology}\) turns
730	  \(\operatorname{IsZero}\) of the homology at \(p\) into \(\operatorname{ExactAt}\) of
731	  the short complex at \(p\). Second, because the degree-\(p\) objects are
732	  \emph{categorical products in \(\mathrm{Ab}\)} rather than \(R\)-modules, the abelian
733	  analogue \(\operatorname{ShortComplex.ab\_exact\_iff}\)
734	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_ab_exact}) --- not the module-category criterion ---
735	  reduces \(\operatorname{ExactAt}\) to the underlying-group exactness
736	  \(\forall x_2,\; g(x_2) = 0 \Rightarrow \exists x_1,\; f(x_1) = x_2\). Third, the
737	  element-level product equivalence
738	  \(\operatorname{ToType}(\prod^{c} F_\sigma) \simeq \prod_\sigma
739	  \operatorname{ToType}(F_\sigma)\) of Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_product_equiv}
740	  identifies each product group with the per-\(\sigma\) localised module \(D^p\), so the
741	  group-level exactness is exactly \(\operatorname{Function.Exact}(D^{p-1} \to D^p \to
742	  D^{p+1})\), supplied by \(\operatorname{dDiff\_exact}\)
743	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_module_exact}).
744	
745	  The identification of the abstract section complex with the concrete localised
746	  complex \(D^\bullet\) --- together with the construction of the prepend transport
747	  \(c\) and the localisation cofaces \(\delta_k\) discharging the unit, shift, and
748	  coface-commutation compatibilities \(\mathtt{hu}\)/\(\mathtt{hsh}\)/\(\mathtt{hcomm}\)
749	  of the dependent combinatorial port --- is the bulk of the bridge. The non-circular
750	  route is reflection along the localisation equivalence on its essential image (the
751	  global-sections functor \(\Gamma\) is the inverse equivalence there, so it reflects
752	  \(\operatorname{IsZero}\) of homology); it is \emph{not} the assertion that
753	  ``\(\Gamma\) preserves homology'', which would presuppose the very affine exactness
754	  being proved.
755	\end{proof}

757	\begin{lemma}
758	
759	[Element-level product equivalence for the section {\v C}ech terms]
760	  \label{lem:section_cech_product_equiv}
761	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCechProductEquiv,
762	    AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCechProductEquiv_apply}
763	  \uses{def:section_cech_complex, def:qcoh_sections_localized}
764	  Let \(\{F_\sigma\}_{\sigma : \mathrm{Fin}(p+1) \to \iota}\) be the finite family of
765	  abelian groups \(F_\sigma = \mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))\) appearing in degree \(p\) of
766	  the section {\v C}ech complex of Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_complex}. There is
767	  an equivalence of underlying types
768	  \[
769	    \operatorname{ToType}\Bigl(\prod^{c}_{\sigma} F_\sigma\Bigr)
770	    \;\simeq\;
771	    \prod_{\sigma} \operatorname{ToType}(F_\sigma),
772	  \]
773	  between the elements of the categorical product \(\prod^{c}_{\sigma} F_\sigma\) in
774	  \(\mathrm{Ab}\) and the dependent functions \(\sigma \mapsto\) (element of
775	  \(F_\sigma\)). It is supplied by the Mathlib concrete-limit comparison
776	  \(\operatorname{CategoryTheory.Limits.Concrete.productEquiv}\), which holds because the
777	  forgetful functor \(\operatorname{forget}\,\mathrm{Ab}\) preserves the discrete
778	  product (\(\operatorname{PreservesLimit}(\operatorname{Discrete.functor} F)\)). Under
779	  this equivalence each coordinate
780	  \(\operatorname{ToType}(\mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma)))\) is identified with the away
781	  localisation \(M_{s_\sigma} = \operatorname{dCoeff}\) of
782	  Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_module_complex} by the
783	  \(\operatorname{IsLocalizedModule}\) uniqueness of
784	  Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized} (both
785	  \(\mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))\) and \(M_{s_\sigma}\) localise \(M\) at \(s_\sigma\), so
786	  the comparison is the canonical isomorphism). This is the move between the
787	  categorical product of \(\mathrm{Ab}\) and the per-\(\sigma\) localised modules.
788	  The coordinate projection of the equivalence is recorded separately as the
789	  definitional restatement
790	  \(\operatorname{sectionCechProductEquiv\_apply}\): the \(\sigma\)-component of the
791	  image of \(y\) under the equivalence is the underlying group map of the categorical
792	  projection \(\pi_\sigma\) applied to \(y\).
793	\end{lemma}
794	
795	\begin{proof}
796	  \uses{def:section_cech_complex, def:qcoh_sections_localized}
797	  The type-level equivalence is \(\operatorname{Concrete.productEquiv}\) applied to the
798	  family \(\sigma \mapsto F_\sigma\); its hypothesis is that
799	  \(\operatorname{forget}\,\mathrm{Ab}\) preserves the discrete-diagram limit, which is
800	  the standard instance for \(\mathrm{Ab}\). The per-coordinate identification
801	  \(\operatorname{ToType}(\mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))) \cong M_{s_\sigma}\) is the
802	  uniqueness clause of \(\operatorname{IsLocalizedModule}\): by
803	  Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized} the restriction map
804	  \(M \to \mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))\) and the localisation unit
805	  \(M \to M_{s_\sigma}\) are both localisations of \(M\) at
806	  \(\operatorname{Submonoid.powers}(s_\sigma)\)
807	  (\(\operatorname{qcohSectionsAwayLocalized}\)), hence canonically isomorphic over
808	  \(M\). Assembling the coordinatewise isomorphisms after the product equivalence gives
809	  the stated identification.

812	\begin{lemma}
813	[Abstract cosimplicial unfold of the section {\v C}ech differential]
814	  \label{lem:section_cech_objd_apply}
815	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCech_objD_apply,
816	    AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCechFaceRestr}
817	  \uses{def:section_cech_complex, lem:section_cech_product_equiv}
818	  Let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a presheaf of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules and
819	  \(\mathcal{U} : X = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) an open cover. For a multi-index
820	  \(\sigma : \mathrm{Fin}(q+2) \to I\) and a face index
821	  \(i \in \mathrm{Fin}(q+2)\), write
822	  \[
823	    \operatorname{sectionCechFaceRestr}(\sigma, i) :
824	      \mathcal{F}\Bigl(\,\bigcap_{l} U_{(\sigma \circ d_i)(l)}\Bigr)
825	      \;\longrightarrow\;
826	      \mathcal{F}\Bigl(\,\bigcap_{k} U_{\sigma(k)}\Bigr)
827	  \]
828	  for the \(i\)-th presheaf face restriction --- the value of \(\mathcal{F}\) on the
829	  inclusion of opens
830	  \(\bigcap_{k} U_{\sigma(k)} \subseteq \bigcap_{l} U_{(\sigma \circ d_i)(l)}\)
831	  obtained from the order-preserving coface \(d_i\) of the simplex category. Then,
832	  read through the product equivalence
833	  \(\operatorname{sectionCechProductEquiv}\) of
834	  Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_product_equiv}, the underlying group action of the
835	  degree-\(q\) differential
836	  \(\operatorname{objD} q\) of the alternating-coface-map complex
837	  \(\operatorname{AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex}
838	  (\operatorname{sectionCechCosimplicial}\,\mathcal{U}\,\mathcal{F})\) is the alternating
839	  sum of the face restrictions applied to the deleted-face coordinates:
840	  \[
841	    \bigl(\operatorname{sectionCechProductEquiv}_{q+1}\,(\operatorname{objD} q\, t)\bigr)_\sigma
842	      \;=\;
843	      \sum_{i = 0}^{q+1} (-1)^i \,
844	        \operatorname{sectionCechFaceRestr}(\sigma, i)
845	          \Bigl(\bigl(\operatorname{sectionCechProductEquiv}_{q}\, t\bigr)_{\sigma \circ d_i}\Bigr).
846	  \]
847	  This is \emph{purely cosimplicial}: it records the shape of the section {\v C}ech
848	  differential as an alternating sum of presheaf restrictions, with \emph{no}
849	  sheaf-theoretic or localisation identification of those restrictions. It is the
850	  abstract shape that the localisation differential \(\operatorname{dDiff}\) of
851	  Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_module_complex} matches once the tilde-bridge
852	  identification of Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_coface_match} is supplied.
853	\end{lemma}
854	
855	\begin{proof}
856	  \uses{lem:section_cech_product_equiv}
857	  Unfold the degree-\(q\) map of \(\operatorname{alternatingCofaceMapComplex}\) on
858	  \(\operatorname{sectionCechCosimplicial}\): by construction \(\operatorname{objD} q\)
859	  is the alternating sum \(\sum_{i} (-1)^i\, d^i\) of the cosimplicial cofaces, each
860	  coface \(d^i\) being the \(\operatorname{Pi.lift}\) whose \(\sigma\)-component
861	  projects to the coordinate \(\sigma \circ d_i\) and post-composes with the presheaf
862	  restriction \(\operatorname{sectionCechFaceRestr}(\sigma, i)\). Reading the
863	  \(\sigma\)-coordinate through the product equivalence (the coordinate-projection
864	  restatement \(\operatorname{sectionCechProductEquiv\_apply}\)) commutes the projection
865	  past the categorical sum: applying the sum of \(\mathrm{Ab}\)-morphisms to an element
866	  distributes over the sum (the finite-sum-application identity
867	  \(\operatorname{ab\_hom\_finsetSum\_apply}\)), and each summand becomes
868	  \((-1)^i\) times the face restriction of the \((\sigma \circ d_i)\)-coordinate. The
869	  per-coordinate projection of the cosimplicial coface is the \(\operatorname{Pi.lift}\)
870	  computation \(\operatorname{Pi.lift\_π}\), which identifies the projected coface with
871	  \(\operatorname{sectionCechFaceRestr}(\sigma, i)\) precomposed with the projection to
872	  \(\sigma \circ d_i\). Assembling the signs yields the stated alternating sum.
873	\end{proof}

878	[Coface match: the section {\v C}ech differential is the localisation differential]
879	  \label{lem:section_cech_coface_match}
880	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCechCofaceMatch}
881	  \uses{lem:section_cech_objd_apply, def:qcoh_sections_localized, def:section_cech_module_complex}
882	  Take \(X = \operatorname{Spec} R\), the standard affine cover \(\mathcal{U}\)
883	  associated with a spanning family \(s : \iota \to R\), and \(\mathcal{F} =
884	  \widetilde{M}\) the quasi-coherent sheaf associated with an \(R\)-module \(M\)
885	  (for a general quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{F}\), see the note following the statement). In
886	  the situation of Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_product_equiv}, the degree-\(p\)
887	  differential of the section {\v C}ech complex
888	  \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})\), read through the product
889	  equivalence, is exactly the localisation differential \(\operatorname{dDiff}\) of the
890	  module complex \(D^\bullet\) of Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_module_complex}.
891	  Concretely, under the per-coordinate identifications each section group
892	  \(\mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))\) is the away localisation \(M_{s_\sigma}\) and each face
893	  restriction \(\operatorname{sectionCechFaceRestr}(\sigma, i)\) is the away-localisation
894	  coface \(\operatorname{dCoface}\), so the alternating sum of face restrictions of
895	  Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_objd_apply} becomes the alternating sum of localisation
896	  cofaces, i.e.\ \(\operatorname{dDiff}\).
897	\end{lemma}

899	% NOTE: For the tilde case \(\mathcal{F} = \widetilde{M}\) the per-coordinate
900	% comparison isomorphism and the naturality square follow directly from
901	% \(\operatorname{qcohRestriction\_eq\_comparison}\). For a general quasi-coherent
902	% \(\mathcal{F}\), reduce to the tilde case via \(\mathcal{F} \cong
903	% \widetilde{\Gamma\mathcal{F}}\) (Stacks Tag 01I8), the affine equivalence
904	% \(\operatorname{QCoh}(\operatorname{Spec} R) \simeq \operatorname{Mod}(R)\),
905	% which is the open gap recorded in Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized}.
906	\begin{proof}
907	  \uses{lem:section_cech_objd_apply, def:section_cech_module_complex, def:qcoh_sections_localized}
908	  \leanok
909	  The proof layers in two steps.
910	
911	  \emph{(i) Abstract unfold.} By Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_objd_apply}, the
912	  \(\sigma\)-coordinate of the degree-\(p\) differential, read through the product
913	  equivalence, is the alternating sum
914	  \(\sum_{i} (-1)^i\, \operatorname{sectionCechFaceRestr}(\sigma, i)\) of presheaf face
915	  restrictions applied to the deleted-face coordinates. This fixes the shape of the
916	  differential without any sheaf identification of the restrictions.
917	
918	  \emph{(ii) Tilde \(\mathcal{F}\)-bridge.} For \(\mathcal{F} = \widetilde{M}\) each
919	  face restriction is identified with the localisation coface
920	  \(\operatorname{SectionCechModule.dCoface}\) by transporting along a per-coordinate
921	  isomorphism. The section group
922	  \(\mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))\) and the localised module
923	  \(M_{s_\sigma} = \operatorname{LocalizedModule}(\operatorname{Submonoid.powers}
924	  s_\sigma)\, M\) both localise \(M\) at the submonoid of powers of
925	  \(s_\sigma = \prod_k s_{\sigma(k)}\): the former by
926	  \(\operatorname{qcohSectionsAwayLocalized}\) together with the multi-index
927	  intersection identity \(\operatorname{basicOpen\_sprod}\) (which rewrites
928	  \(\bigcap_k D(s_{\sigma(k)})\) as the single basic open \(D(s_\sigma)\)), the latter
929	  by construction. Hence there is a canonical \(R\)-linear isomorphism
930	  \[
931	    \varphi_\sigma :
932	      \operatorname{ToType}\bigl(\mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))\bigr)
933	      \;\xrightarrow{\;\sim\;}\;
934	      M_{s_\sigma},
935	  \]
936	  the comparison of the two \(\operatorname{IsLocalizedModule}\) structures, unique by
937	  \(\operatorname{AwayComparison.comparison\_unique}\) and supplied by
938	  \(\operatorname{IsLocalizedModule.iso}\). The naturality square
939	  \[
940	    \varphi_\sigma \;\circ\; \operatorname{sectionCechFaceRestr}(\sigma, i)
941	      \;=\;
942	      \operatorname{dCoface} \;\circ\; \varphi_{\sigma \circ d_i}
943	  \]
944	  is exactly \(\operatorname{qcohRestriction\_eq\_comparison}\) of
945	  Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized}: the presheaf restriction of
946	  \(\widetilde{M}\) between the two section groups is the away-localisation comparison
947	  of the two localisation units. Substituting (ii) into (i) and summing with signs
948	  identifies the section {\v C}ech differential with \(\operatorname{dDiff}\).
949	
950	  The isomorphism \(\varphi_\sigma\) is obtained by matching the two descriptions of
951	  the sections of \(\widetilde{M}\) over \(D(s_\sigma)\): the cosimplicial functor
952	  provides one description and the localisation property of
953	  Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized} provides the other. Their agreement,
954	  together with the naturality square, is the content of this bridge.
955	\end{proof}

1085	  haveI inst1 : ∀ ρ : ↑(Set.range s),
1086	      IsLocalizedModule.Away (↑ρ) (fLoc s M (spanIdx s ρ) (m + 1)) := fun ρ => by
1087	    rw [← spanIdx_spec s ρ]; infer_instance
1088	  haveI inst2 : ∀ ρ : ↑(Set.range s),
1089	      IsLocalizedModule.Away (↑ρ) (fLoc s M (spanIdx s ρ) (m + 2)) := fun ρ => by
1090	    rw [← spanIdx_spec s ρ]; infer_instance
1091	  haveI inst3 : ∀ ρ : ↑(Set.range s),
1092	      IsLocalizedModule.Away (↑ρ) (fLoc s M (spanIdx s ρ) (m + 3)) := fun ρ => by
1093	    rw [← spanIdx_spec s ρ]; infer_instance
1094	  refine exact_of_isLocalized_span (Set.range s) hs
1095	    (fun ρ => ∀ σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι, cechCoeff s M (spanIdx s ρ) σ)
1096	    (fun ρ => fLoc s M (spanIdx s ρ) (m + 1))
1097	    (fun ρ => ∀ σ : Fin (m + 2) → ι, cechCoeff s M (spanIdx s ρ) σ)
1098	    (fun ρ => fLoc s M (spanIdx s ρ) (m + 2))
1099	    (fun ρ => ∀ σ : Fin (m + 3) → ι, cechCoeff s M (spanIdx s ρ) σ)
1100	    (fun ρ => fLoc s M (spanIdx s ρ) (m + 3))
1101	    (dDiff s M (m + 1)) (dDiff s M (m + 2)) fun ρ => ?_
1102	  rw [map_dDiff_eq_locDiff s M (spanIdx s ρ) (m + 1),
1103	    map_dDiff_eq_locDiff s M (spanIdx s ρ) (m + 2)]
1104	  exact locDiff_exact s M (spanIdx s ρ) m
1105	
1106	end SectionCechModule
1107	
1108	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — quasi-coherent sections as away localisations (L1, step (b))
1109	
1110	The categorical→module bridge needs the section-identification of
1111	`def:qcoh_sections_localized`: over a basic open `D(g)` the sections of a
1112	quasi-coherent sheaf are the away localisation `M_g`, and the restriction maps
1113	between basic opens are the canonical localisation comparison maps.  For the
1114	standard sheaf `tilde M` this is *verbatim* from Mathlib's `Tilde` development
1115	(`AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.toOpen` carries `IsLocalizedModule (.powers g)` and the
1116	restriction compatibility is `tilde.toOpen_res`); the only project-local content is
1117	(i) the multi-index intersection `⨅ₖ D(s_{σ k}) = D(s_σ)` identification, which lets
1118	the degree-`p` section group over the `(p+1)`-fold intersection be read as the
1119	localisation `M_{s_σ}` (this is what `lem:section_cech_homology_exact` consumes
1120	degreewise), and (ii) the identification of the abstract presheaf restriction with
1121	`AwayComparison.comparison`, the differential brick.
1122	
1123	For an *arbitrary* quasi-coherent `F` the remaining input is the affine equivalence
1124	`F ≅ tilde(ΓF)` (Stacks 01I8); see `def:qcoh_sections_localized`.  The tilde case
1125	below is the gap-free part that lands the named target. -/
1126	
1127	/-- Splitting an indexed infimum over `Fin (n+1)` into the `0`-th term and the
1128	infimum over the tail.  Lattice-combinatorial helper behind `basicOpen_sprod`. -/
1129	private lemma iInf_fin_succ {α : Type*} [CompleteLattice α] (n : ℕ) (f : Fin (n + 1) → α) :
1130	    (⨅ i, f i) = f 0 ⊓ ⨅ i : Fin n, f i.succ := by
1131	  apply le_antisymm
1132	  · exact le_inf (iInf_le _ 0) (le_iInf fun i => iInf_le _ i.succ)
1133	  · refine le_iInf fun i => ?_
1134	    refine Fin.cases inf_le_left (fun j => le_trans inf_le_right (iInf_le _ j)) i
1135	
1136	/-- **Multi-index basic-open intersection** (geometric input to step (c)): the
1137	`(p+1)`-fold intersection of the basic opens `D(s_{σ k})` of a {\v C}ech multi-index
1138	`σ : Fin n → ι` is the basic open `D(s_σ)` of the product `s_σ = ∏ₖ s_{σ k}`.  This
1139	identifies the section group `F(⨅ₖ D(s_{σ k}))` of `sectionCechCosimplicial` with the
1140	away localisation `M_{s_σ}` of `SectionCechModule.dCoeff`. -/
1141	lemma basicOpen_sprod {R : CommRingCat.{u}} {ι : Type u} (n : ℕ) (s : ι → R) (σ : Fin n → ι) :
1142	    (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s (σ k)) : (Spec R).Opens)
1143	      = PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (∏ k, s (σ k)) := by
1144	  induction n with
1145	  | zero =>
1146	      rw [show (∏ k, s (σ k)) = 1 from Finset.prod_of_isEmpty _]
1147	      simp only [PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen_one]
1148	      exact iInf_of_empty _
1149	  | succ m ih =>
1150	      rw [Fin.prod_univ_succ, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen_mul, ← ih (fun i => σ i.succ),
1151	        iInf_fin_succ]
1152	      rfl
1153	
1154	/-- **Quasi-coherent sections over a {\v C}ech intersection are an away localisation**
1155	(`def:qcoh_sections_localized`, tilde case — step (b) of the L1 bridge).  For the
1156	standard sheaf `tilde M` of an `R`-module `M`, the section-restriction map from `M`
1157	to the sections over the `(p+1)`-fold basic-open intersection
1158	`⨅ₖ D(s_{σ k}) = D(s_σ)` exhibits that section group as the away localisation
1159	`M_{s_σ}` (`IsLocalizedModule` for `Submonoid.powers (∏ₖ s_{σ k})`).  Combines the
1160	Mathlib instance `IsLocalizedModule (.powers g) (tilde.toOpen M (D g)).hom` with the
1161	intersection identification `basicOpen_sprod`.  This is the degreewise section
1162	identification that `lem:section_cech_homology_exact` consumes; the restriction
1163	compatibility (item (5) of the blueprint definition) is `tilde.toOpen_res` together
1164	with `qcohRestriction_eq_comparison` below. -/
1165	lemma qcohSectionsAwayLocalized {R : CommRingCat.{u}} {ι : Type u}
1166	    (M : ModuleCat.{u} R) {n : ℕ} (s : ι → R) (σ : Fin n → ι) :
1167	    IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (∏ k, s (σ k)))
1168	      (AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.toOpen M (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s (σ k)))).hom := by
1169	  rw [basicOpen_sprod]
1170	  infer_instance
1171	
1172	/-- **Restriction = localisation comparison** (`def:qcoh_sections_localized`, item (5);
1173	the differential brick of step (c)).  For the standard sheaf `tilde M`, the presheaf
1174	restriction map between basic-open section groups `M_a → M_b` (along an inclusion
1175	`D(b) ⊆ D(a)`) is, as an `R`-linear map, the canonical away-localisation comparison
1176	`AwayComparison.comparison` — provided `a` acts invertibly on `M_b` (`Inverts a M_b`,
1177	which holds whenever `a ∣ b`, the {\v C}ech-face case).  Proved by the universal
1178	property `AwayComparison.comparison_unique`: both the restriction and the comparison
1179	are `R`-linear maps that recover `tilde.toOpen M (D b)` after precomposition with
1180	`tilde.toOpen M (D a)` (the restriction does so by `tilde.toOpen_res`).  Summed over
1181	the alternating signs, this identifies the section {\v C}ech differential with the
1182	module differential `SectionCechModule.dDiff`. -/
1183	lemma qcohRestriction_eq_comparison {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (M : ModuleCat.{u} R) {a b : R}
1184	    (i : (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen b : (Spec R).Opens) ⟶ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a)
1185	    (hb : AwayComparison.Inverts a
1186	      ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).presheaf.obj
1187	        (Opposite.op (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen b)))) :
1188	    ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).presheaf.map i.op).hom
1189	      = AwayComparison.comparison (AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.toOpen M (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a)).hom
1190	          (AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.toOpen M (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen b)).hom hb := by
1191	  haveI : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers a)
1192	      (AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.toOpen M (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a)).hom := inferInstance
1193	  refine (AwayComparison.comparison_unique
1194	    (AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.toOpen M (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a)).hom
1195	    (AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.toOpen M (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen b)).hom hb _ ?_).symm
1196	  have h := AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.toOpen_res M (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a)
1197	    (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen b) i
1198	  exact congrArg ModuleCat.Hom.hom h
1199	
1200	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — section {\v C}ech homology bridge (L1 steps c, d)
1201	
1202	The categorical→module bridge of `lem:section_cech_homology_exact`: the
1203	`Ab`-valued section {\v C}ech complex `sectionCechComplex` (of `PresheafCech.lean`)
1204	has its degree-`p` object a *categorical product* `∏ᶜ_σ F(⨅ₖ U (σ k))` in `Ab`, and
1205	its differential the alternating sum of the {\v C}ech coface restrictions.  These
1206	lemmas (c1)–(c3) move that abstract complex to the concrete localised-module complex
1207	`SectionCechModule.dDiff` (whose positive-degree exactness `dDiff_exact` is step (a)),
1208	and read off homology vanishing. -/
1209	
1210	section SectionCechBridge
1211	
1212	open CategoryTheory.Limits AlgebraicTopology
1213	
1214	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
1215	
1216	/-- **(c1) Element-level product equivalence** (`lem:section_cech_product_equiv`):
1217	the underlying type of the degree-`p` object `∏ᶜ_σ F(⨅ₖ U (σ k))` of the section
1218	{\v C}ech cosimplicial object is the dependent product `∏_σ ToType (F(⨅ₖ U (σ k)))`.
1219	Supplied by `CategoryTheory.Limits.Concrete.productEquiv` (the forgetful functor of
1220	`Ab` preserves discrete products). -/
1221	noncomputable def sectionCechProductEquiv {ι : Type u} (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
1222	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) (p : ℕ) :
1223	    ToType ((sectionCechCosimplicial U F).obj (SimplexCategory.mk p)) ≃
1224	      (∀ σ : Fin (p + 1) → ι, ToType (F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (σ k))))) :=
1225	  Concrete.productEquiv
1226	    (fun σ : Fin (p + 1) → ι => F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (σ k))))
1227	
1228	/-- Coordinate projection of `sectionCechProductEquiv`: the `σ`-component is the
1229	underlying group map of the categorical projection `Pi.π … σ`.  A definitional
1230	restatement of `Concrete.productEquiv_apply_apply`, named so that downstream proofs can
1231	`rw` it without fighting the `Concrete.productEquiv` coercion. -/
1232	lemma sectionCechProductEquiv_apply {ι : Type u} (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
1233	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) (p : ℕ)
1234	    (y : ToType ((sectionCechCosimplicial U F).obj (SimplexCategory.mk p)))
1235	    (σ : Fin (p + 1) → ι) :
1236	    sectionCechProductEquiv U F p y σ
1237	      = ConcreteCategory.hom
1238	          (Pi.π (fun σ : Fin (p + 1) → ι => F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (σ k)))) σ) y :=
1239	  Concrete.productEquiv_apply_apply _ y σ
1240	
1241	/-- **Homology-to-exactness reduction** (the `Ab`-side of `lem:section_cech_ab_exact`):
1242	the degree-`(q+1)` homology of the section {\v C}ech complex vanishes once the
1243	underlying group homomorphisms of the two consecutive coface differentials
1244	`objD q`, `objD (q+1)` form an exact sequence.  Pure homological algebra: combines
1245	`exactAt_iff_isZero_homology`, `exactAt_iff'`, and the abelian-group exactness
1246	criterion `ShortComplex.ab_exact_iff_function_exact`. -/
1247	lemma sectionCech_isZero_homology_of_objD_exact {ι : Type u}
1248	    (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X) (F : X.PresheafOfModules) (q : ℕ)
1249	    (h : Function.Exact
1250	      (ConcreteCategory.hom (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U F) q))
1251	      (ConcreteCategory.hom
1252	        (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U F) (q + 1)))) :
1253	    IsZero ((sectionCechComplex U F).homology (q + 1)) := by
1254	  rw [← HomologicalComplex.exactAt_iff_isZero_homology,
1255	      (sectionCechComplex U F).exactAt_iff' q (q + 1) (q + 2) (by simp) (by simp),
1256	      ShortComplex.ab_exact_iff_function_exact]
1257	  have hf : (sectionCechComplex U F).d q (q + 1)
1258	      = AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U F) q :=
1259	    CochainComplex.of_d _ _ (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.d_squared _) q
1260	  have hg : (sectionCechComplex U F).d (q + 1) (q + 2)
1261	      = AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U F) (q + 1) :=
1262	    CochainComplex.of_d _ _ (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.d_squared _) (q + 1)
1263	  change Function.Exact
1264	      (ConcreteCategory.hom ((sectionCechComplex U F).d q (q + 1)))
1265	      (ConcreteCategory.hom ((sectionCechComplex U F).d (q + 1) (q + 2)))
1266	  rw [hf, hg]
1267	  exact h
1268	
1269	/-- Application of a finite sum of `Ab`-morphisms distributes over the sum. -/
1270	private lemma ab_hom_finsetSum_apply {A B : Ab.{u}} {κ : Type*}
1271	    (s : Finset κ) (f : κ → (A ⟶ B)) (t : ToType A) :
1272	    ConcreteCategory.hom (∑ i ∈ s, f i) t = ∑ i ∈ s, ConcreteCategory.hom (f i) t := by
1273	  classical
1274	  induction s using Finset.induction with
1275	  | empty => simp
1276	  | insert a s ha ih =>
1277	      rw [Finset.sum_insert ha, Finset.sum_insert ha, AddCommGrpCat.hom_add_apply, ih]
1278	
1279	/-- The `i`-th section {\v C}ech face restriction at a multi-index `σ` of the
1280	cosimplicial section object: the presheaf restriction of `F` from the deleted-face
1281	intersection `⨅ₗ U (σ (δ i l))` to the full intersection `⨅ₖ U (σ k)`.  Factored as a
1282	named `def` so the cosimplicial differential (`sectionCech_objD_apply`) refers to it
1283	by name rather than re-elaborating the `homOfLE (le_iInf …)` term. -/
1284	noncomputable def sectionCechFaceRestr {ι : Type u} (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
1285	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) {q : ℕ} (σ : Fin (q + 2) → ι) (i : Fin (q + 2)) :
1286	    F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (⨅ l, U ((σ ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom) l)))
1287	      ⟶ F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (σ k))) :=
1288	  F.presheaf.map (homOfLE (le_iInf
1289	    (fun l => iInf_le _ ((SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom l)))).op
1290	
1291	/-- **(c2, abstract) Coface match**: the underlying group action of the section
1292	{\v C}ech coface differential `objD q`, read through the product equivalence, is the
1293	alternating sum of the presheaf restriction maps applied to the deleted-face
1294	coordinates — exactly the shape of `SectionCechModule.dDiff`.  This is purely about
1295	the cosimplicial structure of `sectionCechCosimplicial`; no sheaf identification yet. -/
1296	lemma sectionCech_objD_apply {ι : Type u} (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
1297	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) (q : ℕ)
1298	    (t : ToType ((sectionCechCosimplicial U F).obj (SimplexCategory.mk q)))
1299	    (σ : Fin (q + 2) → ι) :
1300	    sectionCechProductEquiv U F (q + 1)
1301	        (ConcreteCategory.hom
1302	          (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U F) q) t) σ
1303	      = ∑ i : Fin (q + 2), (-1 : ℤ) ^ (i : ℕ) •
1304	          ConcreteCategory.hom (sectionCechFaceRestr U F σ i)
1305	            (sectionCechProductEquiv U F q t (σ ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom)) := by
1306	  classical
1307	  rw [sectionCechProductEquiv_apply,
1308	      show ConcreteCategory.hom
1309	            (Pi.π (fun σ : Fin (q + 2) → ι => F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (σ k)))) σ)
1310	            (ConcreteCategory.hom
1311	              (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U F) q) t)
1312	          = ConcreteCategory.hom
1313	              (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U F) q
1314	                ≫ Pi.π (fun σ : Fin (q + 2) → ι => F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (σ k)))) σ) t
1315	          from rfl,
1316	      AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD, Preadditive.sum_comp, ab_hom_finsetSum_apply]
1317	  refine Finset.sum_congr rfl (fun i _ => ?_)
1318	  rw [Preadditive.zsmul_comp]
1319	  simp only [AddCommGrpCat.hom_zsmul, AddMonoidHom.smul_apply]
1320	  rw [sectionCechProductEquiv_apply]
1321	  congr 1
1322	  have hmap : (sectionCechCosimplicial U F).map (SimplexCategory.δ i) ≫ Pi.π _ σ
1323	      = Pi.π _ (σ ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom) ≫ sectionCechFaceRestr U F σ i :=
1324	    Pi.lift_π _ σ
1325	  rw [CosimplicialObject.δ, hmap]
1326	  rfl
1327	
1328	end SectionCechBridge
1329	
1330	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — tilde-bridge and affine vanishing (L1 steps A, B, d)
1331	
1332	The final stretch of the L1 categorical→module bridge for the **tilde sheaf**
1333	`F = ~M`.  The previous section moved the abstract `Ab`-valued section {\v C}ech complex
1334	to the shape of an alternating sum of presheaf restrictions
1335	(`sectionCech_objD_apply`); here we identify, degreewise, those section groups with the
1336	away-localisation coefficients `dCoeff` and the restrictions with the localisation
1337	cofaces `dCoface`, via the per-σ comparison `φ_σ` (`IsLocalizedModule.iso`).  Transporting
1338	the already-proven module exactness `SectionCechModule.dDiff_exact` across the resulting
1339	degreewise additive isomorphism (`Function.Exact.of_ladder_addEquiv_of_exact`) discharges
1340	the underlying-group exactness, which `sectionCech_isZero_homology_of_objD_exact` turns
1341	into positive-degree homology vanishing of the section {\v C}ech complex
1342	(`sectionCech_affine_vanishing`). -/
1343	
1344	section SectionCechTilde
1345	
1346	open AlgebraicTopology Scheme.Modules SectionCechModule CechLocalized AwayComparison
1347	
1348	variable {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (M : ModuleCat.{u} R) {ι : Type u} (s : ι → R)
1349	
1350	set_option maxHeartbeats 800000 in
1351	-- raised: the `IsLocalizedModule.ext` reduction over the heavy `modulesSpecToSheaf` section
1352	-- types is defeq-intensive (the accessor-2 `~M` sections do not reduce cheaply).
1353	/-- **Linear-level per-coface naturality** (`R`-linear).  The comparison `φ_σ`
1354	(written here as the raw `IsLocalizedModule.iso ▸ symm`, definitionally `phiL` below)
1355	intertwines the (accessor-2) {\v C}ech face restriction with the away-localisation coface
1356	`dCoface`.  Proved by `IsLocalizedModule.ext`: both composites send the localisation unit
1357	`toOpen M (D s_{σ∘dᵢ})` of `M` to the localisation unit `mkLinearMap (powers s_σ)`, by
1358	`tilde.toOpen_res` (restriction commutes with `toOpen`), `iso_symm_comp`, and
1359	`comparison_apply` (the defining property of `dCoface`).  Stated and proved *before* the
1360	`phiL` abbreviation so its `IsLocalizedModule.ext` elaboration is not slowed by
1361	fold/unfold attempts against `phiL` — keeping it within the heartbeat budget. -/
1362	private lemma phiL_naturality {q : ℕ} (σ : Fin (q + 2) → ι) (i : Fin (q + 2)) :
1363	    haveI := qcohSectionsAwayLocalized M s σ
1364	    (IsLocalizedModule.iso (Submonoid.powers (∏ k, s (σ k)))
1365	        (tilde.toOpen M (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s (σ k)))).hom).symm.toLinearMap ∘ₗ
1366	        ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).presheaf.map
1367	          (homOfLE (le_iInf (fun l => iInf_le _
1368	            ((SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom l)))).op).hom
1369	      = (dCoface s M (q + 1) σ i) ∘ₗ
1370	        (haveI := qcohSectionsAwayLocalized M s (σ ∘ i.succAbove)
1371	         (IsLocalizedModule.iso (Submonoid.powers (∏ k, s ((σ ∘ i.succAbove) k)))
1372	          (tilde.toOpen M (⨅ k,
1373	            PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s ((σ ∘ i.succAbove) k)))).hom).symm.toLinearMap) := by
1374	  haveI i1 := qcohSectionsAwayLocalized M s σ
1375	  haveI i2 := qcohSectionsAwayLocalized M s (σ ∘ i.succAbove)
1376	  -- Abstract the concrete face-restriction map as an opaque `g` BEFORE `IsLocalizedModule.ext`,
1377	  -- so `ext` never whnf's the (heartbeat-heavy) `modulesSpecToSheaf` restriction.  `hg` records
1378	  -- its `toOpen` compatibility (`tilde.toOpen_res`).
1379	  set g := ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).presheaf.map
1380	      (homOfLE (le_iInf (fun l => iInf_le _
1381	        ((SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom l)))).op).hom with hg_def
1382	  have hg : ∀ m : M,
1383	      g ((tilde.toOpen M (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s ((σ ∘ i.succAbove) k)))).hom m)
1384	        = (tilde.toOpen M (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s (σ k)))).hom m := by
1385	    intro m
1386	    rw [hg_def]
1387	    exact congrFun (congrArg (fun l => (l.hom : _ → _))
1388	      (tilde.toOpen_res M (⨅ l, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen
1389	          (s ((σ ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom) l)))
1390	        (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s (σ k)))
1391	        (homOfLE (le_iInf (fun l => iInf_le _ ((SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom l)))))) m
1392	  clear_value g
1393	  refine IsLocalizedModule.ext (Submonoid.powers (∏ k, s ((σ ∘ i.succAbove) k)))
1394	    (tilde.toOpen M (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s ((σ ∘ i.succAbove) k)))).hom
1395	    (fun x => (Inverts.of_dvd (sprod_succAbove_dvd s σ i)
1396	      (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (sprod s σ)) M)).isUnit_powers x) ?_
1397	  apply LinearMap.ext
1398	  intro m
1399	  -- unfold the `LinearEquiv`-coercion compositions to plain applications (defeq), avoiding
1400	  -- the semilinear-instance mismatch that blocks `rw`/`simp` on `↑(iso).symm ∘ₗ g`
1401	  change (IsLocalizedModule.iso (Submonoid.powers (∏ k, s (σ k)))
1402	      (tilde.toOpen M (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s (σ k)))).hom).symm
1403	        (g ((tilde.toOpen M (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s ((σ ∘ i.succAbove) k)))).hom m))
1404	      = dCoface s M (q + 1) σ i
1405	        ((IsLocalizedModule.iso (Submonoid.powers (∏ k, s ((σ ∘ i.succAbove) k)))
1406	          (tilde.toOpen M (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s ((σ ∘ i.succAbove) k)))).hom).symm

1480	        (⨅ l, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s ((σ ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom) l)))))) :

1483	  have hb := restr_bridge M
1484	    (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s (σ k)))
1485	    (⨅ l, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s ((σ ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom) l)))

1494	private noncomputable def sectionProdAddEquiv (p : ℕ) :
1495	    ToType ((sectionCechCosimplicial (tU s) (tP M)).obj (SimplexCategory.mk p))
1496	      ≃+ (∀ σ : Fin (p + 1) → ι,

1520	    sectionToModuleAddEquiv M s p y σ = phi M s σ (sectionCechProductEquiv (tU s) (tP M) p y σ) :=

1525	private lemma sectionProdEquiv_symm_apply (q : ℕ) (z : ∀ σ : Fin (q + 1) → ι, dCoeff s M σ)
1526	    (τ : Fin (q + 1) → ι) :
1527	    sectionCechProductEquiv (tU s) (tP M) q ((sectionToModuleAddEquiv M s q).symm z) τ
1528	      = (phi M s τ).symm (z τ) := by
1529	  have h : sectionCechProductEquiv (tU s) (tP M) q ((sectionToModuleAddEquiv M s q).symm z)
1530	      = sectionProdAddEquiv M s q ((sectionProdAddEquiv M s q).symm
1531	          ((AddEquiv.piCongrRight (fun σ => phi M s σ)).symm z)) := rfl
1532	  rw [h, AddEquiv.apply_symm_apply]
1533	  rfl

1541	    sectionToModuleAddEquiv M s (q + 1)
1542	        (ConcreteCategory.hom
1543	          (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial (tU s) (tP M)) q)
1544	          ((sectionToModuleAddEquiv M s q).symm z))
1545	      = dDiff s M (q + 1) z := by
1546	  funext σ
1547	  rw [sectionToModuleAddEquiv_apply, sectionCech_objD_apply, dDiff_apply, map_sum]
1548	  refine Finset.sum_congr rfl (fun i _ => ?_)

1550	  exact congrArg ((-1 : ℤ) ^ (i : ℕ) • dCoface s M (q + 1) σ i ·)
1551	    (AddEquiv.apply_symm_apply (phi M s (σ ∘ i.succAbove)) (z (σ ∘ i.succAbove)))

1557	`Function.Exact.of_ladder_addEquiv_of_exact`. -/
1558	private lemma sectionCechAbExact [Finite ι] (hs : Ideal.span (Set.range s) = ⊤) (q : ℕ) :
1559	    Function.Exact
1560	      (ConcreteCategory.hom
1561	        (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial (tU s) (tP M)) q))

1563	        (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial (tU s) (tP M)) (q + 1))) := by
1564	  have sq : ∀ r : ℕ,
1565	      (ConcreteCategory.hom
1566	            (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial (tU s) (tP M)) r)).comp
1567	          (sectionToModuleAddEquiv M s r).symm.toAddMonoidHom
1568	        = (sectionToModuleAddEquiv M s (r + 1)).symm.toAddMonoidHom.comp
1569	            (dDiff s M (r + 1)).toAddMonoidHom := by
1570	    intro r
1571	    apply AddMonoidHom.ext

1578	    (sectionToModuleAddEquiv M s q).symm (sectionToModuleAddEquiv M s (q + 1)).symm

1587	theorem sectionCech_homology_exact {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (M : ModuleCat.{u} R)
1588	    {ι : Type u} [Finite ι] (s : ι → R) (hs : Ideal.span (Set.range s) = ⊤)

1599	quasi-coherent `F` is reduced to this tilde case via `F ≅ ~(ΓF)` (Stacks 01I8, deferred). -/
1600	theorem sectionCech_affine_vanishing {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (M : ModuleCat.{u} R)

957	\begin{lemma}
958	[Abelian-group exactness criterion for the section {\v C}ech complex]
959	  \label{lem:section_cech_ab_exact}
960	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCechAbExact,
961	    AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCech_isZero_homology_of_objD_exact,
962	    AlgebraicGeometry.ab_hom_finsetSum_apply}
963	  \uses{def:section_cech_complex, lem:section_cech_objd_apply,
964	    lem:section_cech_product_equiv, lem:section_cech_coface_match,
965	    lem:section_cech_module_exact}
966	  For \(1 \leq p\) the homology of the section {\v C}ech complex
967	  \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})\) of
968	  Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_complex} vanishes,
969	  \(\operatorname{IsZero}(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U},
970	  \mathcal{F}).\mathrm{homology}\, p)\). The argument splits into a homological
971	  precursor and an unconditional ladder transport.
972	  \begin{itemize}
973	    \item The precursor \(\operatorname{sectionCech\_isZero\_homology\_of\_objD\_exact}\)
974	      is pure homological algebra: \emph{given}
975	      \(\operatorname{Function.Exact}\) of the underlying group homomorphisms of two
976	      consecutive coface differentials \(\operatorname{objD} q\),
977	      \(\operatorname{objD}(q+1)\), the homology of the section complex at degree
978	      \(q+1\) is zero. It chains
979	      \(\operatorname{exactAt\_iff\_isZero\_homology}\),
980	      \(\operatorname{HomologicalComplex.exactAt\_iff'}\) (to expose the short complex
981	      around the node), and the abelian-group exactness criterion
982	      \(\operatorname{ShortComplex.ab\_exact\_iff\_function\_exact}\) (which states that
983	      a short complex of abelian groups is exact iff for every \(x_2\) with
984	      \(g(x_2) = 0\) there is \(x_1\) with \(f(x_1) = x_2\)).
985	    \item The unconditional \(\operatorname{sectionCechAbExact}\) discharges that
986	      hypothesis by transporting the already-proved \(R\)-module exactness
987	      \(\operatorname{dDiff\_exact}\) of
988	      Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_module_exact} across the degreewise additive
989	      isomorphism, using the ladder-transport lemma
990	      \(\operatorname{Function.Exact.of\_ladder\_addEquiv\_of\_exact}\).
991	  \end{itemize}
992	\end{lemma}
993	
994	\begin{proof}
995	  \uses{lem:section_cech_objd_apply, lem:section_cech_product_equiv,
996	    lem:section_cech_coface_match, lem:section_cech_module_exact}
997	  Fix \(p = q + 1 \geq 1\). By the precursor
998	  \(\operatorname{sectionCech\_isZero\_homology\_of\_objD\_exact}\) it suffices to
999	  exhibit \(\operatorname{Function.Exact}\) of the underlying group homomorphisms of the
1000	  two consecutive coface differentials \(\operatorname{objD} q\),
1001	  \(\operatorname{objD}(q+1)\) of the section complex.
1002	
1003	  This exactness is obtained by the ladder transport
1004	  \(\operatorname{Function.Exact.of\_ladder\_addEquiv\_of\_exact}\). The vertical maps
1005	  of the ladder are the degreewise additive isomorphisms
1006	  \(e_p = \prod_\sigma \varphi_\sigma \circ \operatorname{sectionCechProductEquiv}_p\):
1007	  the product equivalence of Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_product_equiv} followed by the
1008	  coordinatewise comparison \(\varphi_\sigma\) identifying each section group with the
1009	  localised module \(M_{s_\sigma}\). The two commuting squares of the ladder are the
1010	  coface match: the abstract unfold of the differential as an alternating sum of face
1011	  restrictions (Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_objd_apply}, the decl
1012	  \(\operatorname{sectionCech\_objD\_apply}\)) together with the per-coordinate
1013	  identification of each face restriction with the localisation coface
1014	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_coface_match}) shows
1015	  \(e_{p+1} \circ \operatorname{objD} p = \operatorname{dDiff}^{\,p} \circ e_p\). The
1016	  horizontal exactness fed into the transport is the \(R\)-module exactness
1017	  \(H = \operatorname{dDiff\_exact}\) of Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_module_exact}.
1018	  Transporting \(H\) across the additive ladder yields the desired
1019	  \(\operatorname{Function.Exact}\) of the \(\operatorname{objD}\) maps, discharging the
1020	  hypothesis of the precursor.
1021	\end{proof}
1022	
1023	\begin{definition}
1024	[The un-localised section {\v C}ech module complex \(D^\bullet\)]
1025	  \label{def:section_cech_module_complex}
1026	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.SectionCechModule.dCoeff,
1027	    AlgebraicGeometry.SectionCechModule.dCoface,
1028	    AlgebraicGeometry.SectionCechModule.dDiff,
1029	    AlgebraicGeometry.SectionCechModule.dDiff_apply,
1030	    AlgebraicGeometry.SectionCechModule.dToCech,
1031	    AlgebraicGeometry.SectionCechModule.dToCech_isLocalizedModule,
1032	    AlgebraicGeometry.SectionCechModule.cechCoface_dToCech,
1033	    AlgebraicGeometry.SectionCechModule.dToCech_comm,
1034	    AlgebraicGeometry.SectionCechModule.cechCofaceLin,
1035	    AlgebraicGeometry.SectionCechModule.cechCoface_apply,
1036	    AlgebraicGeometry.SectionCechModule.locDiff,
1037	    AlgebraicGeometry.SectionCechModule.locDiff_apply,
1038	    AlgebraicGeometry.SectionCechModule.locDiff_eq_depDiff,
1039	    AlgebraicGeometry.SectionCechModule.locDiff_exact,
1040	    AlgebraicGeometry.SectionCechModule.fLoc,
1041	    AlgebraicGeometry.SectionCechModule.fLoc_apply,
1042	    AlgebraicGeometry.SectionCechModule.fLoc_isLocalizedModule,
1043	    AlgebraicGeometry.SectionCechModule.locDiff_fLoc,
1044	    AlgebraicGeometry.SectionCechModule.map_dDiff_eq_locDiff,
1045	    AlgebraicGeometry.SectionCechModule.spanIdx,
1046	    AlgebraicGeometry.SectionCechModule.spanIdx_spec,
1047	    AlgebraicGeometry.AwayComparison.comparison_isLocalizedModule,
1048	    AlgebraicGeometry.AwayComparison.Inverts.smul_pow_cancel}
1049	  \uses{def:standard_affine_cover, def:qcoh_sections_localized}
1050	  Let \(R\) be a commutative ring, \(M = \Gamma(\operatorname{Spec} R, \mathcal{F})\)
1051	  the global sections of a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_{\operatorname{Spec}
1052	  R}\)-module \(\mathcal{F}\), and \(s : \iota \to R\) (\(\iota\) finite) a spanning
1053	  family of the unit ideal, \(\operatorname{Ideal.span}(\operatorname{range} s) =
1054	  \top\) (Definition~\ref{def:standard_affine_cover}). The \emph{un-localised section
1055	  {\v C}ech module complex} \(D^\bullet\) is the cochain complex of \(R\)-modules
1056	  whose degree-\(p\) term is the product over multi-indices
1057	  \(\sigma : \{0, \ldots, p\} \to \iota\) of the away localisations
1058	  \[
1059	    D^p \;=\; \prod_{\sigma} M_{s_\sigma},
1060	    \qquad s_\sigma = \prod_{k} s_{\sigma(k)},
1061	    \qquad M_{s_\sigma} = \operatorname{LocalizedModule}
1062	      \bigl(\operatorname{Submonoid.powers}(s_\sigma)\bigr)\, M,
1063	  \]
1064	  with differential \(d^p : D^p \to D^{p+1}\) the alternating sum
1065	  \(\sum_{j} (-1)^j\, \delta^j\) of the coface comparison maps \(\delta^j\), each of
1066	  which is the canonical away-localisation comparison
1067	  \(M_{s_{\sigma \circ d_j}} \to M_{s_\sigma}\) sending a fraction to its image under
1068	  the further localisation at the omitted factor. The single-coefficient comparison
1069	  map and its localisation property are recorded by the \(\mathtt{dCoface}\) and
1070	  \(\mathtt{dCoeff}\) data and the differential by \(\mathtt{dDiff}\) (with action
1071	  \(\mathtt{dDiff\_apply}\)); the auxiliary \(\mathtt{spanIdx}\)/\(\mathtt{spanIdx\_spec}\)
1072	  select a witnessing index of the spanning relation.
1073	
1074	  The complex \(D^\bullet\) is tied to the section {\v C}ech complex of
1075	  Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_complex} and to its localisations by three
1076	  comparison families. First, the maps \(\mathtt{dToCech}\) identify each term
1077	  \(D^p\) with the degree-\(p\) section group
1078	  \(\prod_\sigma \mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))\) of
1079	  Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized} as a localised module
1080	  (\(\mathtt{dToCech\_isLocalizedModule}\)), intertwining the differentials

1125	  Exactness of \(D^\bullet\) at each positive-degree node is a local-to-global
1126	  statement over the spanning family. By the local-to-global exactness criterion
1127	  \(\operatorname{exact\_of\_isLocalized\_span}(\operatorname{Set.range} s)\) (with the
1128	  spanning hypothesis \(\operatorname{Ideal.span}(\operatorname{range} s) = \top\)), it
1129	  suffices to verify \(\operatorname{Function.Exact}(d^{p+1}, d^{p+2})\) after
1130	  localising at each spanning element \(s_r\). In the localisation at \(s_r\) the
1131	  comparison family \(\mathtt{map\_dDiff\_eq\_locDiff}\) identifies the localised
1132	  differential of \(D^\bullet\) with the localised Čech differential \(\mathtt{locDiff}\)

1134	  transitivity \(M_a[1/b] = M_{ab}\) of
1135	  \(\mathtt{AwayComparison.comparison\_isLocalizedModule}\)). Exactness of that
1136	  localised differential is \(\mathtt{locDiff\_exact}\), which is in turn the

1140	  giving the localised exactness one localisation at a time. Reassembling over the
1141	  spanning family yields the un-localised exactness.

1315	\subsection{Presheaf-level {\v C}ech machinery}
1316	\label{sec:presheaf_cech_machinery}
1317	
1318	The {\v C}ech-acyclicity of injective \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules
1319	(Lemma~\ref{lem:injective_cech_acyclic} below) is not a statement about a single
1320	affine cover: it is proved on the abelian category \(\mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\)
1321	of presheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules, where the {\v C}ech complex of sections
1322	is the \(\operatorname{Hom}\)-complex of an explicit free resolution. The argument
1323	rests on two presheaf-level facts, recorded here, and one transfer of injectivity.
1324	First, a free complex \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet\) of presheaves of modules whose
1325	\(\operatorname{Hom}\)-complex into \(\mathcal{F}\) \emph{is} the {\v C}ech complex
1326	of sections (Definition~\ref{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex},
1327	Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_complex} and
1328	Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_complex_hom_identification}). Second, the resolution property of
1329	that complex: \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet\) resolves \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}\)
1330	concentrated in degree \(0\) (Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_free_complex_quasi_iso}). Together
1331	these give: an injective \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module is injective as a presheaf
1332	(Lemma~\ref{lem:injective_cech_acyclic} via
1333	Lemma~\ref{lem:injective_of_adjoint} applied to the sheafification adjunction
1334	Lemma~\ref{lem:mod_pmod_adjunction}), so \(\operatorname{Hom}(-, \mathcal{I})\) is
1335	exact and carries the augmented resolution to an exact section complex, i.e.\
1336	\(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{I}) = 0\) for \(p > 0\). This direct route uses
1337	neither enough-injectives in \(\mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\) nor a right-derived
1338	\(\delta\)-functor identification.
1339	
1340	\begin{definition}

1377	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology,
1378	  \texttt{lemma-cech-map-into} (the free complex \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet\)).}
1379	  Let \(X\) be a ringed space and \(\mathcal{U} : U = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) an
1380	  open covering, with \(j_{i_0 \ldots i_p} : U_{i_0 \ldots i_p} \hookrightarrow X\)
1381	  the open immersion of each finite intersection. The Stacks formulation uses, for
1382	  each intersection \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\), the \emph{extension-by-zero} presheaf
1383	  \((j_{i_0 \ldots i_p})_{!}\,\mathcal{O}_X|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}}\), the presheaf of
1384	  modules sending an open \(W\) to \(\mathcal{O}_X(W)\) when
1385	  \(W \subset U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\) and to \(0\) otherwise. This extension-by-zero
1386	  presheaf is, canonically, the \emph{free presheaf of modules on the representable}
1387	  \(\operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,U_{i_0 \ldots i_p})\): writing
1388	  \(\mathbf{y}\,V = \operatorname{Hom}(-, V)\) for the Yoneda presheaf of the open
1389	  \(V\) on the site of opens of \(X\), the free presheaf of modules
1390	  \(\operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,V)\) sends \(W\) to the free
1391	  \(\mathcal{O}_X(W)\)-module on the set \(\operatorname{Hom}(W, V)\), which is
1392	  \(\mathcal{O}_X(W)\) if \(W \subset V\) and \(0\) otherwise — exactly
1393	  extension-by-zero of \(\mathcal{O}_X|_V\). Its defining universal property
1394	  \[
1395	    \operatorname{Hom}_{\mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)}\bigl(
1396	      \operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,V),\, \mathcal{F}\bigr)
1397	    \;\cong\; \mathcal{F}(V)
1398	  \]
1399	  is the free–forgetful adjunction for presheaves of modules composed with the
1400	  Yoneda isomorphism \(\operatorname{Hom}(\mathbf{y}\,V, G) \cong G(V)\). The
1401	  \emph{free presheaf complex} of \(\mathcal{U}\) is the chain complex
1402	  \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet\) in the abelian category
1403	  \(\mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\) of presheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules whose
1404	  term in degree \(p\) is the direct sum
1405	  \[
1406	    K(\mathcal{U})_p
1407	      = \bigoplus_{(i_0, \ldots, i_p) \in I^{p+1}}
1408	        \operatorname{free}\bigl(\mathbf{y}\,U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\bigr),
1409	  \]
1410	  with \(K(\mathcal{U})_p\) placed in degree \(p \geq 0\) (and \(0\) in negative
1411	  degrees), and whose differential \(K(\mathcal{U})_{p+1} \to K(\mathcal{U})_p\) is
1412	  the alternating sum \(\sum_{j=0}^{p+1} (-1)^j\) of the maps
1413	  \(\operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,U_{i_0 \ldots i_{p+1}}) \to
1414	  \operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,U_{i_0 \ldots \widehat{i_j} \ldots i_{p+1}})\)
1415	  obtained by applying \(\operatorname{free}\) to the representable index-dropping
1416	  maps \(\mathbf{y}\,U_{i_0 \ldots i_{p+1}} \to \mathbf{y}\,U_{i_0 \ldots
1417	  \widehat{i_j} \ldots i_{p+1}}\) induced by the inclusions of opens
1418	  \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_{p+1}} \subset U_{i_0 \ldots \widehat{i_j} \ldots i_{p+1}}\)
1419	  that drop the index \(i_j\). Each summand is a \emph{projective} object of
1420	  \(\mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\), because the universal property above identifies
1421	  \(\operatorname{Hom}_{\mathcal{O}_X}(\operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,U_{i_0 \ldots
1422	  i_p}), -)\) with the exact ``sections over \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\)'' functor
1423	  \((-)(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p})\); this is the structural feature exploited below.
1424	
1425	  We assume the index set \(I\) is \emph{finite} (so the required coproducts exist in
1426	  \(\mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\); this matches the finiteness hypothesis of the
1427	  comparison target). Here the direct sum \(\bigoplus\) is the categorical coproduct
1428	  \(\coprod\) (the indexed \(\operatorname{Sigma}\) colimit), which for a finite index
1429	  set coincides with the finite biproduct.
1430	\end{definition}

1432	\begin{definition}
1433	
1434	[Section {\v C}ech complex of a presheaf of modules]
1435	  \label{def:section_cech_complex}
1436	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCechComplex,
1437	    AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCechCosimplicial}
1438	  \uses{def:cech_complex}
1439	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology, \S{\v C}ech cohomology of presheaves,
1440	  % definition of $\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})$
1441	  % (read from references/stacks-cohomology.tex, L879--910).
1442	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Set
1443	  % $$
1444	  % \check{\mathcal{C}}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})
1445	  % =
1446	  % \prod\nolimits_{(i_0, \ldots, i_p) \in I^{p + 1}}
1447	  % \mathcal{F}(U_{i_0\ldots i_p}).
1448	  % $$
1449	  % This is an abelian group. ... We define
1450	  % $$
1451	  % d : \check{\mathcal{C}}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})
1452	  % \longrightarrow
1453	  % \check{\mathcal{C}}^{p + 1}(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})
1454	  % $$
1455	  % by the formula
1456	  % $$
1457	  % d(s)_{i_0\ldots i_{p + 1}}
1458	  % =
1459	  % \sum\nolimits_{j = 0}^{p + 1}
1460	  % (-1)^j
1461	  % s_{i_0\ldots \hat i_j \ldots i_{p + 1}}|_{U_{i_0\ldots i_{p + 1}}}
1462	  % $$
1463	  % It is straightforward to see that $d \circ d = 0$."
1464	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology, \S{\v C}ech cohomology of presheaves.}
1465	  Let \(X\) be a ringed space and \(\mathcal{U} : U = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) an open
1466	  covering. For a presheaf of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules \(\mathcal{F}\), the
1467	  \emph{section {\v C}ech complex} \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U},
1468	  \mathcal{F})\) is the cochain complex of \emph{abelian groups} whose term in
1469	  degree \(p\) is the product of sections over the \((p+1)\)-fold intersections,
1470	  \[
1471	    \check{\mathcal{C}}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})
1472	      = \prod_{(i_0, \ldots, i_p) \in I^{p+1}}
1473	        \mathcal{F}\bigl(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\bigr),
1474	  \]
1475	  each factor being the value of the ``sections over \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\)'' functor
1476	  (the evaluation of \(\mathcal{F}\) at the open \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\)), with
1477	  differential the alternating sum of restriction maps
1478	  \[
1479	    d(s)_{i_0 \ldots i_{p+1}}
1480	      = \sum_{j=0}^{p+1} (-1)^j\,
1481	        s_{i_0 \ldots \widehat{i_j} \ldots i_{p+1}}
1482	        \big|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_{p+1}}}.
1483	  \]
1484	  This object is \emph{distinct} from the relative {\v C}ech complex
1485	  \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F})\) of
1486	  Definition~\ref{def:cech_complex}: the latter is the pushforward complex with terms
1487	  \(\prod (f|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}})_*(\mathcal{F}|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}})\) in
1488	  \(\operatorname{QCoh}(S)\), an object of relative sheaves over the base, whereas the
1489	  section {\v C}ech complex here is the simpler complex of \emph{global sections} of
1490	  \(\mathcal{F}\) over the intersections, an object of abelian groups (the underlying
1491	  additive structure of the sections). The two must not be conflated: the
1492	  presheaf-level acyclicity argument below runs entirely on this section complex.
1493	\end{definition}

1496	[The free complex represents the {\v C}ech complex]

1510	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology, Lemma \texttt{lemma-cech-map-into}
1511	  % (statement + proof) (read from references/stacks-cohomology.tex,
1512	  % L1163--1196).
1513	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Then there is an isomorphism
1514	  % $$
1515	  % \Hom_{\mathcal{O}_X}(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet, \mathcal{F})
1516	  % =
1517	  % \check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})
1518	  % $$
1519	  % functorial in $\mathcal{F} \in \Ob(\textit{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X))$."
1520	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology,
1521	  \texttt{lemma-cech-map-into}.}
1522	  Let \(X\) be a ringed space and \(\mathcal{U} : U = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) an
1523	  open covering. For every presheaf of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules \(\mathcal{F}\)
1524	  there is an isomorphism of cochain complexes of \emph{abelian groups}
1525	  \[
1526	    \operatorname{Hom}_{\mathcal{O}_X}\bigl(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet, \mathcal{F}\bigr)
1527	      \;=\;
1528	    \check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}),
1529	  \]
1530	  natural in \(\mathcal{F} \in \mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\). Here the left side is
1531	  the cochain complex obtained by applying the contravariant
1532	  \(\operatorname{Hom}_{\mathcal{O}_X}(-, \mathcal{F})\) to the chain complex
1533	  \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet\) of Definition~\ref{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex},
1534	  and the right side is the section {\v C}ech complex of
1535	  Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_complex}.

1538	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "We saw in the discussion just above the lemma that
1539	% $$
1540	% \Hom_{\mathcal{O}_X}(
1541	% (j_{i_0\ldots i_p})_{p!}\mathcal{O}_{U_{i_0\ldots i_p}},
1542	% \mathcal{F})
1543	% =
1544	% \mathcal{F}(U_{i_0\ldots i_p}).
1545	% $$
1546	% Hence we see that it is indeed the case that the direct sum
1547	% $$
1548	% \bigoplus\nolimits_{i_0 \ldots i_p}
1549	% (j_{i_0 \ldots i_p})_{p!}\mathcal{O}_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}}
1550	% $$
1551	% represents the functor
1552	% $$
1553	% \mathcal{F}
1554	% \longmapsto
1555	% \prod\nolimits_{i_0\ldots i_p} \mathcal{F}(U_{i_0\ldots i_p}).
1556	% $$
1557	% Hence by Categories, Yoneda Lemma \ref{categories-lemma-yoneda}
1558	% we see that there is a complex $K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet$ with terms
1559	% as given. It is a simple matter to see that the maps are as given
1560	% in the lemma."
1561	\begin{proof}
1562	  \uses{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex, def:section_cech_complex}
1563	  The free–forgetful adjunction for presheaves of modules gives, composed with the
1564	  Yoneda isomorphism \(\operatorname{Hom}(\mathbf{y}\,V, \mathcal{F}) \cong
1565	  \mathcal{F}(V)\), the natural identification, for each multi-index,
1566	  \[
1567	    \operatorname{Hom}_{\mathcal{O}_X}\bigl(
1568	      \operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}),\, \mathcal{F}
1569	    \bigr)
1570	    \;\cong\;
1571	    \operatorname{Hom}\bigl(\mathbf{y}\,U_{i_0 \ldots i_p},\,
1572	      \mathcal{F}\bigr)
1573	    \;\cong\;
1574	    \mathcal{F}(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}),
1575	  \]
1576	  where the last group is the evaluation of \(\mathcal{F}\) at the open
1577	  \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\). Taking the product over multi-indices,
1578	  \(\operatorname{Hom}_{\mathcal{O}_X}(K(\mathcal{U})_p, \mathcal{F}) =
1579	  \prod_{i_0 \ldots i_p} \mathcal{F}(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}) =
1580	  \check{\mathcal{C}}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})\); the direct sum
1581	  \(K(\mathcal{U})_p\) therefore represents the functor \(\mathcal{F} \mapsto
1582	  \prod_{i_0 \ldots i_p} \mathcal{F}(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p})\). Under this
1583	  identification the \((-1)^j\) maps \(\operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,U_{i_0
1584	  \ldots i_{p+1}}) \to \operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,U_{i_0 \ldots \widehat{i_j}
1585	  \ldots i_{p+1}})\) defining the differential of \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet\) become
1586	  exactly the alternating-sum restriction differential of
1587	  \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})\), so the two cochain
1588	  complexes coincide; naturality in \(\mathcal{F}\) is immediate from the
1589	  naturality of the free–forgetful adjunction and of Yoneda. The per-multi-index
1590	  bijection \(\operatorname{Hom}_{\mathcal{O}_X}(\operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,
1591	  U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}), \mathcal{F}) \cong \mathcal{F}(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p})\) is additive,
1592	  so it is promoted to an isomorphism of abelian groups (an \emph{additive}
1593	  equivalence); taking the product over multi-indices and assembling these degreewise
1594	  additive isomorphisms — which commute with the alternating-sum differentials — yields
1595	  the complex isomorphism by matching components degree by degree.
1596	\end{proof}

1621	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology, \texttt{lemma-homology-complex}.}
1622	  Let \(X\) be a ringed space and \(\mathcal{U} : U = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) an open
1623	  covering. The \emph{cover structure presheaf} \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}} :
1624	  X.\mathrm{PresheafOfModules}\) is the image presheaf of the augmentation

1631	  \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}(W) = \mathcal{O}_X(W)\) when \(W\) is contained in some
1632	  \(U_i\), and \(0\) otherwise. The degree-\(0\) term
1633	  \(K(\mathcal{U})_0 = \coprod_i \operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,U_i)\) of the free
1634	  presheaf complex of Definition~\ref{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex} carries the

1639	\begin{lemma}
1640	[Objectwise detection of quasi-isomorphisms of presheaf-of-module complexes]
1641	  \label{lem:quasiIso_of_evaluation}
1642	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.quasiIso_of_evaluation,
1643	    AlgebraicGeometry.isIso_Fmap_homologyMap,
1644	    AlgebraicGeometry.isIso_of_evaluation}
1645	  \uses{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex}
1646	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology, Lemma \texttt{lemma-homology-complex}
1647	  % (proof, the sectionwise-reduction step) (read from
1648	  % references/stacks-cohomology.tex, L1220--1226).
1649	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "It suffices to show that taking sections of this extended
1650	  % complex over any open $W \subset X$ leads to an acyclic complex."
1651	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology, \texttt{lemma-homology-complex}
1652	  (sectionwise reduction).}
1653	  Let \(R\) be a presheaf of rings on a category \(C\) and let
1654	  \(\varphi : K \to L\) be a morphism of chain complexes in the abelian category
1655	  \(\mathrm{PMod}(R)\) of presheaves of \(R\)-modules. Suppose that for \emph{every}
1656	  object \(V\) the evaluated morphism — the image of \(\varphi\) under the functor
1657	  \(\bigl(\operatorname{PresheafOfModules.evaluation} R\, V\bigr).
1658	  \operatorname{mapHomologicalComplex}\), a morphism of complexes of
1659	  \(R(V)\)-modules — is a quasi-isomorphism. Then \(\varphi\) is a
1660	  quasi-isomorphism. This is the objectwise-reduction step that turns the
1661	  resolution claim of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_free_complex_quasi_iso} into a family of
1662	  sectionwise (combinatorial) statements: homology in \(\mathrm{PMod}(R)\) is
1663	  computed open-by-open, because the evaluation functors
1664	  \(\operatorname{PresheafOfModules.evaluation} R\, V\) are exact (hence preserve
1665	  homology) and jointly conservative. The Lean entry point is
1666	  \(\operatorname{quasiIso\_of\_evaluation}\), which combines the joint
1667	  conservativity of the evaluations (each \(g\) is an isomorphism as soon as every
1668	  \((\operatorname{evaluation} R\, V).\operatorname{map} g\) is) with the fact that
1669	  each \(\operatorname{PresheafOfModules.evaluation} R\, V\) preserves homology.
1670	\end{lemma}
1671	
1672	\begin{proof}
1673	  \uses{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex}
1674	  A morphism of complexes is a quasi-isomorphism iff it induces an isomorphism on
1675	  homology in every degree (\(\operatorname{quasiIso\_iff}\),
1676	  \(\operatorname{quasiIsoAt\_iff\_isIso\_homologyMap}\)). Fix a degree \(i\). The
1677	  homology map \(H_i(\varphi)\) is an isomorphism in \(\mathrm{PMod}(R)\) iff each of
1678	  its evaluations is an isomorphism, since the underlying
1679	  presheaf-of-abelian-groups functor reflects isomorphisms and a morphism of
1680	  presheaves is an isomorphism iff it is so objectwise (joint conservativity). For
1681	  each \(V\), evaluation preserves homology, so the evaluation of \(H_i(\varphi)\)
1682	  is the homology map of the evaluated complex morphism; the latter is an
1683	  isomorphism because the evaluated morphism is a quasi-isomorphism by hypothesis.
1684	  Hence \(H_i(\varphi)\) is an isomorphism for all \(i\), i.e.\ \(\varphi\) is a
1685	  quasi-isomorphism.
1686	\end{proof}

1688	\begin{lemma}
1689	
1690	[Sectionwise description of the evaluated free complex]
1691	  \label{lem:cech_free_eval_sectionwise}
1692	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cechFreeEval_X,
1693	    AlgebraicGeometry.freeYonedaEval_iso_of_le,
1694	    AlgebraicGeometry.freeYonedaEval_isZero_of_not_le}
1695	  \uses{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex, def:cover_structure_presheaf}
1696	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology, Lemma \texttt{lemma-homology-complex}
1697	  % (proof, the sectionwise description of $K^{ext}_\bullet(W)$) (read from
1698	  % references/stacks-cohomology.tex, L1227--1252).
1699	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Write $I = I_1 \amalg I_2$ where $W \subset U_i$ if and only
1700	  % if $i \in I_1$. ... If $I_1 \not = \emptyset$, then
1701	  % $\mathcal{O}_\mathcal{U}(W) = \mathcal{O}_X(W)$ and
1702	  % $K^{ext}_p(W) = \bigoplus\nolimits_{i_0 \ldots i_p \in I_1} \mathcal{O}_X(W)$.
1703	  % This is true because of the simple description of the presheaves
1704	  % $(j_{i_0 \ldots i_p})_{p!}\mathcal{O}_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}}$."
1705	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology, \texttt{lemma-homology-complex}
1706	  (description of the evaluated complex).}
1707	  Fix an open \(V \subset X\) and write \(I_1 = I_1(V) = \{\,i \in I : V \leq U_i\,\}\)
1708	  for the set of cover members containing \(V\). Evaluating the free presheaf complex
1709	  \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet\) of Definition~\ref{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex} at
1710	  \(V\) gives, degreewise,
1711	  \[
1712	    K(\mathcal{U})_p(V)
1713	      = \bigoplus_{\sigma : \mathrm{Fin}(p+1) \to I}
1714	        \operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,U_\sigma)(V)
1715	      = \bigoplus_{\substack{\sigma : \mathrm{Fin}(p+1) \to I \\ V \leq U_\sigma}}
1716	        \mathcal{O}_X(V)
1717	      = \bigoplus_{\sigma : \mathrm{Fin}(p+1) \to I_1} \mathcal{O}_X(V),
1718	  \]
1719	  because \(\operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,W)(V) = \mathcal{O}_X(V)\) when
1720	  \(V \leq W\) and \(0\) otherwise (poset hom-set is a subsingleton), and
1721	  \(V \leq U_\sigma = U_{\sigma(0)} \cap \cdots \cap U_{\sigma(p)}\) holds iff
1722	  \(\sigma\) takes all its values in \(I_1\). Under this identification the
1723	  differential of \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet(V)\) is the alternating-sum combinatorial
1724	  {\v C}ech differential of the full simplex on the index set \(I_1\) with
1725	  coefficients in the single module \(\mathcal{O}_X(V)\). Moreover the evaluated
1726	  augmentation target is \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}(V) = \mathcal{O}_X(V)\) when
1727	  \(I_1 \neq \varnothing\) and \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}(V) = 0\) when
1728	  \(I_1 = \varnothing\). Thus the evaluation at \(V\) of the augmented complex
1729	  \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet \to \mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}[0]\)
1730	  (\(\operatorname{cechFreeComplexAug}\)) is precisely the augmented combinatorial
1731	  {\v C}ech complex of the simplex on \(I_1\) with coefficients \(\mathcal{O}_X(V)\).
1732	  The Lean entry point for the degree-\(p\) unfolding is
1733	  \(\operatorname{cechFreePresheafComplex\_X}\) (which exposes
1734	  \(K(\mathcal{U})_p = \coprod_\sigma \operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,U_\sigma)\) by
1735	  \(\operatorname{rfl}\)) composed with
1736	  \(\operatorname{PresheafOfModules.evaluation}\) at \(V\). The full degreewise
1737	  identification is assembled from three pieces: \(\operatorname{cechFreeEval\_X}\)
1738	  (evaluation at \(V\) commutes with the coproduct, giving
1739	  \((\operatorname{eval} V)(K(\mathcal{U})_p) \cong \coprod_\sigma
1740	  (\operatorname{eval} V)(\operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,U_\sigma))\)),
1741	  \(\operatorname{freeYonedaEval\_iso\_of\_le}\) (a surviving summand with
1742	  \(V \leq U_\sigma\) is \(\cong \mathcal{O}_X(V)\)), and
1743	  \(\operatorname{freeYonedaEval\_isZero\_of\_not\_le}\) (a summand with
1744	  \(V \not\leq U_\sigma\) is the zero object); collapsing the coproduct over the
1745	  surviving \(\sigma : \mathrm{Fin}(p+1) \to I_1\) gives
1746	  \(\bigoplus_{\sigma : \mathrm{Fin}(p+1) \to I_1} \mathcal{O}_X(V)\).
1747	\end{lemma}
1748	
1749	\begin{proof}
1750	  \uses{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex, def:cover_structure_presheaf}
1751	  The degreewise identity is the value at \(V\) of the explicit description of the
1752	  free presheaf \(\operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,W)\) recorded in
1753	  Definition~\ref{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex}: it sends \(V\) to the free
1754	  \(\mathcal{O}_X(V)\)-module on \(\operatorname{Hom}(V, W)\), which is
1755	  \(\mathcal{O}_X(V)\) if \(V \leq W\) and \(0\) otherwise. Applying
1756	  \(\operatorname{PresheafOfModules.evaluation}\;V\) to the coproduct decomposition
1757	  \(\operatorname{cechFreePresheafComplex\_X}\) commutes with the direct sum and
1758	  collapses each summand to \(\mathcal{O}_X(V)\) or \(0\) according to whether
1759	  \(V \leq U_\sigma\), i.e.\ whether \(\sigma\) factors through \(I_1\); the surviving
1760	  summands are exactly those indexed by \(\sigma : \mathrm{Fin}(p+1) \to I_1\). The
1761	  evaluated faces are the index-dropping restriction maps, which on the surviving
1762	  summands are identities of \(\mathcal{O}_X(V)\), so the alternating sum is the
1763	  combinatorial {\v C}ech differential. The augmentation value follows from
1764	  \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}(V) = \mathcal{O}_X(V)\) exactly when some \(U_i\)
1765	  contains \(V\) (\(I_1 \neq \varnothing\)), recorded in
1766	  Definition~\ref{def:cover_structure_presheaf}.
1767	\end{proof}

2212	\begin{lemma}[Right adjoint of a mono-preserving left adjoint preserves injectives]
2213	  \label{lem:injective_of_adjoint}
2214	  \lean{CategoryTheory.Injective.injective_of_adjoint}
2215	  \mathlibok
2216	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib
2217	  (\texttt{Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Preadditive.Injective.Basic}).}
2218	  Let \(L \dashv R\) be an adjunction between abelian (or, more generally, suitable)
2219	  categories such that the left adjoint \(L\) preserves monomorphisms. Then for every
2220	  injective object \(J\) of the target, \(R(J)\) is an injective object of the source.
2221	\end{lemma}

2409	
2410	  \emph{Part 1: an injective sheaf is injective as a presheaf.} Sheafification is
2411	  left adjoint to the inclusion
2412	  \(\iota : \mathrm{Mod}(\mathcal{O}_X) \hookrightarrow \mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\)
2413	  of sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules into presheaves of

2415	  adjoint sheafification is exact, hence preserves monomorphisms. By

2418	  \(\mathcal{I}\) is injective as an object of \(\mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\). In
2419	  particular the contravariant functor \(\operatorname{Hom}_{\mathcal{O}_X}(-,
2420	  \mathcal{I})\) is exact on \(\mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\).

2423	  presheaf complex \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet\) of

2436	  \mathcal{I}(U)\) supplying degree \(0\).

2440	  \mathcal{U}\) is a quasi-isomorphism (Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_free_complex_quasi_iso});

2442	  applying \(\operatorname{preadditiveYoneda}.\mathrm{obj}\,\mathcal{I}\) keeps it a
2443	  quasi-isomorphism, since \(\mathcal{I}\) is injective as a presheaf of modules
2444	  (Part 1) and \(\operatorname{Hom}(-, \mathcal{I})\) then preserves homology
2445	  (\(\operatorname{quasiIso\_map\_preadditiveYoneda\_of\_injective}\),
2446	  Lemma~\ref{lem:hom_into_injective_exact}). Transporting source and target of this
2447	  mapped-opposite quasi-isomorphism across the opposite-transport isos
2448	  \(\operatorname{homCechComplexMapOpIso}\)

2450	  \(\operatorname{sectionCechComplexMapOpIso}\)
2451	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_complex_mapop_iso}) identifies it with the augmentation
2452	  of the section {\v C}ech complex \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U},
2453	  \mathcal{I})\) onto the mapped image of the degree-\(0\)-concentrated target

2456	  \(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{I}) = 0\) for all \(p > 0\) and

2458	  neither enough-injectives in \(\mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\) nor a right-derived
2459	  \(\delta\)-functor identification.
2460	\end{proof}
